syntax_10800.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10800.title: World Creator
syntax_10800.description:
Create a new world creator. This will be used to create a new world.
After creating a world creator you will need to load the world.

**Name/Key**:
 - Name: This is the name of your new world (As of Minecraft 26.1, names are discouraged and keys are suggested.
 - Key: This is the key of your new world, and is used to identify it.
   - As of Minecraft 26.1, the key will determine where your world folder is saved within the `world/dimensions` folder.

**Other**:
 - Copy will create a fresh new world with the same seed and settings.
 - Clone will create a carbon copy of your world, if the world is large, this process may take a while. The file copying process will happen on another thread, so this won't freeze your server.
 - `without saving` = Optionally prevent saving of the world that will be cloned. Saving freezes the server for a bit so this can help speed up the process... if you don't need that world saved at this moment in time.
syntax_10800.syntax_pattern:
[a] [new] world creator (with name|named) %string%
[a] [new] world creator with key %namespacedkey/string%
[a] [new] world creator (with name|named) %string% to (copy|clone) %world% [without saving]
[a] [new] world creator with key %namespacedkey/string% to (copy|clone) %world% [without saving]

en:
Create a new world creator. This will be used to create a new world.
After creating a world creator you will need to load the world.

**Name/Key**:
 - Name: This is the name of your new world (As of Minecraft 26.1, names are discouraged and keys are suggested.
 - Key: This is the key of your new world, and is used to identify it.
   - As of Minecraft 26.1, the key will determine where your world folder is saved within the `world/dimensions` folder.

**Other**:
 - Copy will create a fresh new world with the same seed and settings.
 - Clone will create a carbon copy of your world, if the world is large, this process may take a while. The file copying process will happen on another thread, so this won't freeze your server.
 - `without saving` = Optionally prevent saving of the world that will be cloned. Saving freezes the server for a bit so this can help speed up the process... if you don't need that world saved at this moment in time.

syntax_11567.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11567.title: ChunkGenerator - ChunkData Biome
syntax_11567.description:
Represents the biome in ChunkData.
The first pattern is used to set the biome in the `biome gen` section.
The second pattern is used to retrieve biomes in the `chunk gen` and `block pop` sections.
NOTE: The vector represents the position of the biome in the chunk, not the world.

en:
Represents the biome in ChunkData.
The first pattern is used to set the biome in the `biome gen` section.
The second pattern is used to retrieve biomes in the `chunk gen` and `block pop` sections.
NOTE: The vector represents the position of the biome in the chunk, not the world.

syntax_11919.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11919.title: Open Real Inventory
syntax_11919.description:
Open real inventories to players.
This will open a real inventory object instead of a custom inventory object to players.
Most of these (except enchanting and workbench) require a PaperMC server.
`at %location%` is used to open an inventory at an actual block (Optional, will default to player's location).
Some inventories require a location of a block for extra functionality,such as an enchanting table uses nearby bookshelves to determine enchantment level.
syntax_11919.syntax_pattern:
open real anvil [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real cartography [table] [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real enchanting [table] [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real grindstone [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real loom [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real smithing [table] [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real stonecutter [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%
open real workbench [inventory] [at %-location%] [named %-string%] to %players%

en:
Open real inventories to players.
This will open a real inventory object instead of a custom inventory object to players.
Most of these (except enchanting and workbench) require a PaperMC server.
`at %location%` is used to open an inventory at an actual block (Optional, will default to player's location).
Some inventories require a location of a block for extra functionality,such as an enchanting table uses nearby bookshelves to determine enchantment level.

syntax_11924.syntax_pattern:

%itemstacks/itemtypes/slots%'[s] repair cost component

en:
The number of experience levels to add to the base level cost when repairing, combining, or renaming this item with an anvil.
Must be a non-negative integer, defaults to 0.

syntax_10538.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10538.title: Recipe - Crafting Recipe
syntax_10538.description: Register a new shaped/shapeless recipe for a specific item using custom ingredients. Recipes support items and material choices for ingredients. Material choices allow you to use Minecraft tags or lists of items. The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores, NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:", this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players. You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book. NOTE: Recipes with 4 or less ingredients will be craftable in the player's crafting grid.
syntax_10538.syntax_pattern: register [new] (shaped|shapeless) recipe for %itemtype% (using|with ingredients) %itemtypes/recipechoices% with id %string% [in group %-string%]

en: Register a new shaped/shapeless recipe for a specific item using custom ingredients. Recipes support items and material choices for ingredients. Material choices allow you to use Minecraft tags or lists of items. The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores, NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:", this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players. You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book. NOTE: Recipes with 4 or less ingredients will be craftable in the player's crafting grid.

syntax_11822.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11822.title: ItemComponent - Tool Component Apply
syntax_11822.description:
Apply a tool component to any item making it usable tool.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Tool Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tool) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `default_mining_speed` = The default mining speed of this tool, used if no rules override it. Defaults to 1.0. [Optional]
- `damage_per_block` = The amount of durability to remove each time a block is broken with this tool. Must be a non-negative integer. [Optional]
- `can_destroy_blocks_in_creative` = Whether players can break blocks while holding this tool in Creative mode. Defaults to true. [Optional] (Requires MC 1.21.5+)
- `rules:` =  A list of rules for the blocks that this tool has a special behavior with.

en:
Apply a tool component to any item making it usable tool.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Tool Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tool) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `default_mining_speed` = The default mining speed of this tool, used if no rules override it. Defaults to 1.0. [Optional]
- `damage_per_block` = The amount of durability to remove each time a block is broken with this tool. Must be a non-negative integer. [Optional]
- `can_destroy_blocks_in_creative` = Whether players can break blocks while holding this tool in Creative mode. Defaults to true. [Optional] (Requires MC 1.21.5+)
- `rules:` =  A list of rules for the blocks that this tool has a special behavior with.

syntax_11076.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11076.title: Scoreboard - Objective Number Format
syntax_11076.description:
Represents the way an objective score can be formatted.
There are three format types: Blank (nothing there), Fixed (a string of your choosing) and Styled (colored numbers).
NOTES:
`set` = You can set the format to any string you'd like (fixed) or a json component (styled number).
`delete` = Will leave it blank (nothing will be there, just nothing).
`reset` = Will reset back to its original format.
See [**Json Formatting**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Raw_JSON_text_format) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.20.4+

en:
Represents the way an objective score can be formatted.
There are three format types: Blank (nothing there), Fixed (a string of your choosing) and Styled (colored numbers).
NOTES:
`set` = You can set the format to any string you'd like (fixed) or a json component (styled number).
`delete` = Will leave it blank (nothing will be there, just nothing).
`reset` = Will reset back to its original format.
See [**Json Formatting**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Raw_JSON_text_format) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.20.4+

syntax_11860.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11860.title: Bound - Locations
syntax_11860.description:
Get/modify the locations of a bound.
Greater will always equal the higher south-east corner. 
Lesser will always equal the lower north-west corner.
You can set the two corners of the bound to new values.
You can also add/subtract vectors to/from the corners.

en:
Get/modify the locations of a bound.
Greater will always equal the higher south-east corner. 
Lesser will always equal the lower north-west corner.
You can set the two corners of the bound to new values.
You can also add/subtract vectors to/from the corners.

syntax_14447.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_14447.title: Dialog - Dynamic Callback Action Button
syntax_14447.description:
Add a dynamic action button to a dialog.
This action includes a callback section to run code when the action button is clicked.
See [**Custom Dynmaic Action**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Dialog#dynamic/custom) on McWiki for more specific info.
**Entries**:
- `label` = The name on your button, accepts a string or text component/mini message.
- `tooltip` = The hover message, accepts a string or text component/mini message.
- `width` = The width of the button. Value between 1 and 1024 — Defaults to 150.
- `trigger` = This section will run code when the button is clicked.

**Callback Section Event-Values**:
- `event-nbt` = Returns NBT from the event (will contain data from inputs).
- `event-audience` = The audience represented in this event.
- `event-playerconnection` = The player connection represented in this event (Used if opening a dialog in the async player connection config evnet).
- `event-player` = The player represented in this event (Might be null if the player isn't available yet, such as in the async config event).
- `event-string` = The name of the player/player connection represented in this event (Useful if the player is null).
- `event-uuid` - The uuid of the player/player connection represented in this event.
syntax_14447.entries: [{"name": "label", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "tooltip", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "width", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "trigger", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}]

en:
Add a dynamic action button to a dialog.
This action includes a callback section to run code when the action button is clicked.
See [**Custom Dynmaic Action**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Dialog#dynamic/custom) on McWiki for more specific info.
**Entries**:
- `label` = The name on your button, accepts a string or text component/mini message.
- `tooltip` = The hover message, accepts a string or text component/mini message.
- `width` = The width of the button. Value between 1 and 1024 — Defaults to 150.
- `trigger` = This section will run code when the button is clicked.

**Callback Section Event-Values**:
- `event-nbt` = Returns NBT from the event (will contain data from inputs).
- `event-audience` = The audience represented in this event.
- `event-playerconnection` = The player connection represented in this event (Used if opening a dialog in the async player connection config evnet).
- `event-player` = The player represented in this event (Might be null if the player isn't available yet, such as in the async config event).
- `event-string` = The name of the player/player connection represented in this event (Useful if the player is null).
- `event-uuid` - The uuid of the player/player connection represented in this event.

syntax_10878.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10878.title: Recipe - Register Shaped Recipe
syntax_10878.description:
This section allows you to register a shaped recipe, define the shape and set ingredients.
You can optionally add a group and category.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting item of this recipe.
 - `shape` = A list of strings (1 to 3 strings) which each have 1-3 characters (must be the same char count per string). These correspond to the ingredients set for these shapes. (See examples for details.) Blank spaces will just be empty spaces in a crafting grid.
 - `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) (optional).
 - `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in (optional).Options are "building", "redstone", "equipment", "misc".
 - `ingredients` = This section is where you will set the ingredients to correspend with your shape.
syntax_10878.entries: [{"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "result", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "shape", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "group", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "category", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "ingredients", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}]

en:
This section allows you to register a shaped recipe, define the shape and set ingredients.
You can optionally add a group and category.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting item of this recipe.
 - `shape` = A list of strings (1 to 3 strings) which each have 1-3 characters (must be the same char count per string). These correspond to the ingredients set for these shapes. (See examples for details.) Blank spaces will just be empty spaces in a crafting grid.
 - `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) (optional).
 - `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in (optional).Options are "building", "redstone", "equipment", "misc".
 - `ingredients` = This section is where you will set the ingredients to correspend with your shape.

syntax_10545.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10545.title: Server Tick - Step Server
syntax_10545.description:
Steps the game a certain amount of ticks if the server is currently frozen.
Steps occur when the server is in a frozen state which can be started by either using
the in game `/tick freeze` command or the `server frozen state` expression.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Steps the game a certain amount of ticks if the server is currently frozen.
Steps occur when the server is in a frozen state which can be started by either using
the in game `/tick freeze` command or the `server frozen state` expression.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_14495.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14495.title: Dust Option
syntax_14495.description:
Creates a new dust option to be used with 'dust' particle. Color can either be a regular color or an RGB color using
Skript's rgb() function. Size is the size the particle will be.

syntax_11061.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11061.title: Player Interact
syntax_11061.description:
Called when a player interacts (clicks) a block or entity.
This is similar to Skript's click event, but more verbose giving you more freedom.
Note: This event may be called once for each hand.
`event-vector` = 
`event-equipmentslot` = .
`event-blockaction` = .
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-blockface`
 - `event-equipment slot`
   - **Description**: The slot (hand_slot, off_hand_slot) used to click (may be null).
 - `event-block action`
   - **Description**: The action that happened, such as (left_click_air, physical, right_click_block).
 - `event-vector`
   - **Description**: An offset from the location of the clicked block/entity.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-item stack`
 - `event-block`
 - `event-direction`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity`
syntax_11061.event_values: event-block, event-block action, event-blockface, event-direction, event-entity, event-equipment slot, event-item stack, event-location, event-player, event-vector, event-world

en:
Called when a player interacts (clicks) a block or entity.
This is similar to Skript's click event, but more verbose giving you more freedom.
Note: This event may be called once for each hand.
`event-vector` = 
`event-equipmentslot` = .
`event-blockaction` = .
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-blockface`
 - `event-equipment slot`
   - **Description**: The slot (hand_slot, off_hand_slot) used to click (may be null).
 - `event-block action`
   - **Description**: The action that happened, such as (left_click_air, physical, right_click_block).
 - `event-vector`
   - **Description**: An offset from the location of the clicked block/entity.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-item stack`
 - `event-block`
 - `event-direction`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity`

syntax_10593.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10593.title: BossBar - Create
syntax_10593.description:
Create your own custom BossBar.
**NOTE**: Progress is a number between 0-100.
**NOTE**: This just creates a new custom bossbar. It will by default not be visible to anyone
until you actually add players to it. See examples!!!
**NOTE**: The ID is optional. If excluded, the BossBar will not save to the server.
syntax_10593.syntax_pattern: [new] boss[ ]bar [(named|with id) %-string%] with title %string% [with (color|colour) %-color%] [with style %-bossbarstyle%] [with progress %-number%]

en:
Create your own custom BossBar.
**NOTE**: Progress is a number between 0-100.
**NOTE**: This just creates a new custom bossbar. It will by default not be visible to anyone
until you actually add players to it. See examples!!!
**NOTE**: The ID is optional. If excluded, the BossBar will not save to the server.

syntax_11939.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11939.title: Break Blocks with Effects
syntax_11939.description:
Breaks blocks as if a player had broken them. Will drop items, play particles and sounds. Requires PaperMC.
Optionally you can trigger it to drop experience as well.
Optionally you can include an item which is used to determine which drops the block will drop.

en:
Breaks blocks as if a player had broken them. Will drop items, play particles and sounds. Requires PaperMC.
Optionally you can trigger it to drop experience as well.
Optionally you can include an item which is used to determine which drops the block will drop.

syntax_10511.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10511.title: Chunk - Load/Unload
syntax_10511.description:
Load or unload a chunk.
**Options**:
- `%number%,[ ]%number%` = Represents the X/Z coords of a chunk. Not to be confused with a location. Chunk coords are essentially a location divided by 16, example: Chunk 1/1 = Location 16/16
- `async` = Will load the chunk off the main thread (Requires PaperMC). Your code will halt whilst waiting for the chunk to load.
- `with ticket` = Will add a ticket to the chunk, preventing it from unloading until you explicitly unload it or the server stops.
- `without saving` = Will prevent the chunk from saving when unloading.
syntax_10511.syntax_pattern:
[async] load chunk at %number%,[ ]%number% (in|of) [world] %world% [with ticket]
unload chunk at %number%,[ ]%number% (in|of) [world] %world%
[async] load chunk at %location% [with ticket]
unload chunk at %location% [without saving]
unload %chunks% [without saving]

en:
Load or unload a chunk.
**Options**:
- `%number%,[ ]%number%` = Represents the X/Z coords of a chunk. Not to be confused with a location. Chunk coords are essentially a location divided by 16, example: Chunk 1/1 = Location 16/16
- `async` = Will load the chunk off the main thread (Requires PaperMC). Your code will halt whilst waiting for the chunk to load.
- `with ticket` = Will add a ticket to the chunk, preventing it from unloading until you explicitly unload it or the server stops.
- `without saving` = Will prevent the chunk from saving when unloading.

syntax_10533.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10533.title: Recipe - Cooking Recipe
syntax_10533.description:
Register a new cooking recipe for furnaces, blast furnaces, smokers, and campfires.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "skbee:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book.
syntax_10533.syntax_pattern: register [new] (furnace|(blast furnace|blasting)|smok(er|ing)|campfire) recipe for %itemtype% (using|with ingredient) %itemtype/recipechoice% with id %string% [[and ]with exp[erience] %-number%] [[and ]with cook[ ]time %-timespan%] [in group %-string%]

en:
Register a new cooking recipe for furnaces, blast furnaces, smokers, and campfires.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "skbee:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book.

syntax_11858.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11858.title: Attribute Modifier Properties
syntax_11858.description:
Represents the different components of an attribute modifier.
NOTE: These cannot be changed.
`id` = The unique NamespacedKey of a modifier.
`amount` = Amount of change from the modifier.
`slot` = Equipment Slot Group the item must be in for the modifier to take effect.
`operation` = The operation of a modifier. See [**McWiki**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Attribute#Operations) for more details.

en:
Represents the different components of an attribute modifier.
NOTE: These cannot be changed.
`id` = The unique NamespacedKey of a modifier.
`amount` = Amount of change from the modifier.
`slot` = Equipment Slot Group the item must be in for the modifier to take effect.
`operation` = The operation of a modifier. See [**McWiki**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Attribute#Operations) for more details.

syntax_10778.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10778.title: TextComponent - Item Name
syntax_10778.description:
Get/set the component name of an Item.
`(custom|display) name` = Get/set the `custom_name` component of an item just like you would with Skript's name expression.
`item name` = Get/set the `item_name` component of an item (Requires Minecraft 1.20.5+).
Unlike setting the custom/display name of an item, this name cannot be changed through an anvil,
and does not show in some labels, such as banner markers and item frames.
See [**McWiki**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#item_name) for more details.

en:
Get/set the component name of an Item.
`(custom|display) name` = Get/set the `custom_name` component of an item just like you would with Skript's name expression.
`item name` = Get/set the `item_name` component of an item (Requires Minecraft 1.20.5+).
Unlike setting the custom/display name of an item, this name cannot be changed through an anvil,
and does not show in some labels, such as banner markers and item frames.
See [**McWiki**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#item_name) for more details.

syntax_10689.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10689.title: NBT - Compound of Object
syntax_10689.description:
Get the NBT compound of a block/entity/item/file/chunk.

**SPECIAL CASES**:
- `full nbt of %item%` = Returns a copy of the FULL NBT of an item (this includes id, count and 'components' compound).
Modifying this will have no effect on the original item. This is useful for serializing items.
- `nbt of %item%` = Returns the original. Modifying this will modify the original item.
\- This will return the 'components' portion of an item's full NBT.
- `custom nbt of %item%` = Returns the 'minecraft:custom_data' component of an item's NBT. Modifying this will modify the original item.
Please see [**Data Component Format**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format) on McWiki for more information on item NBT components.
- `[full] vanilla nbt of %item%` = Will return the same as above except it will include vanilla components which don't normally show in NBT.
- `nbt copy of %objects%` = Returns a copy of the original NBT compound. This way you can modify it without
actually modifying the original NBT compound, for example when grabbing the compound from an entity, modifying it and applying to other entities.
- `custom nbt of %object%` = Returns the custom portion of the NBT of the object.
For many objects Minecraft does not allow for custom NBT, so they're stored in various spots:
  - Entity: Stored in the entity's PersistentDataContainer.
  - Item: Stored in the item's `minecraft:custom_data` component.
  - Block (TileEntity - such as furnace, chest): Stored in the block's PersistentDataContainer.
  - Block (non TileEntity): Stored in the PersistentDataContainer of the chunk the block is in.

NBT from a file will need to be saved manually using
the 'NBT - Save File' effect. If the file does not yet exist, a new file will be created.

CHANGERS:
- `add` = Adding a compound to another compound will merge them (This is controlled by Minecraft, results may vary).
- `delete` = Will delete NBT files, or clear the NBT compound (This can break entities/players, be careful!).
- `reset` = Will clear the NBT compound (This can break entities/players, be careful!)

en:
Get the NBT compound of a block/entity/item/file/chunk.

**SPECIAL CASES**:
- `full nbt of %item%` = Returns a copy of the FULL NBT of an item (this includes id, count and 'components' compound).
Modifying this will have no effect on the original item. This is useful for serializing items.
- `nbt of %item%` = Returns the original. Modifying this will modify the original item.
\- This will return the 'components' portion of an item's full NBT.
- `custom nbt of %item%` = Returns the 'minecraft:custom_data' component of an item's NBT. Modifying this will modify the original item.
Please see [**Data Component Format**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format) on McWiki for more information on item NBT components.
- `[full] vanilla nbt of %item%` = Will return the same as above except it will include vanilla components which don't normally show in NBT.
- `nbt copy of %objects%` = Returns a copy of the original NBT compound. This way you can modify it without
actually modifying the original NBT compound, for example when grabbing the compound from an entity, modifying it and applying to other entities.
- `custom nbt of %object%` = Returns the custom portion of the NBT of the object.
For many objects Minecraft does not allow for custom NBT, so they're stored in various spots:
  - Entity: Stored in the entity's PersistentDataContainer.
  - Item: Stored in the item's `minecraft:custom_data` component.
  - Block (TileEntity - such as furnace, chest): Stored in the block's PersistentDataContainer.
  - Block (non TileEntity): Stored in the PersistentDataContainer of the chunk the block is in.

NBT from a file will need to be saved manually using
the 'NBT - Save File' effect. If the file does not yet exist, a new file will be created.

CHANGERS:
- `add` = Adding a compound to another compound will merge them (This is controlled by Minecraft, results may vary).
- `delete` = Will delete NBT files, or clear the NBT compound (This can break entities/players, be careful!).
- `reset` = Will clear the NBT compound (This can break entities/players, be careful!)

syntax_10681.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10681.title: Merchant Recipe - Create
syntax_10681.description:
Create a merchant recipe.

NOTE: You will need to use the merchant recipe ingredients expression to add ingredients.

max uses = A trade has a maximum number of uses. A Villager may periodically replenish it's trades
by resetting the uses of it's merchant recipes to 0, allowing them to be used again.

experience reward = A trade may or may not reward experience for being completed.

Demand & Special Price were added in MC 1.18.x:

demand = This value is periodically updated by the villager that owns this merchant recipe based on
how often the recipe has been used since it has been last restocked in relation to its maximum uses.
The amount by which the demand influences the amount of the first ingredient is scaled by the recipe's
price multiplier, and can never be below zero.

special price =  This value is dynamically updated whenever a player starts and stops trading with a
villager that owns this merchant recipe. It is based on the player's individual reputation with the villager,
and the player's currently active status effects (ex: hero of the village).
The influence of the player's reputation on the special price is scaled by the recipe's price multiplier.
syntax_10681.syntax_pattern: [new] merchant recipe with result %itemtype% with max uses %number% [with uses %-number%] [(|with experience reward)] [with villager experience %-number%] [with price multiplier %-number%] [with demand %-number%] [with special price %-number%]

en:
Create a merchant recipe.

NOTE: You will need to use the merchant recipe ingredients expression to add ingredients.

max uses = A trade has a maximum number of uses. A Villager may periodically replenish it's trades
by resetting the uses of it's merchant recipes to 0, allowing them to be used again.

experience reward = A trade may or may not reward experience for being completed.

Demand & Special Price were added in MC 1.18.x:

demand = This value is periodically updated by the villager that owns this merchant recipe based on
how often the recipe has been used since it has been last restocked in relation to its maximum uses.
The amount by which the demand influences the amount of the first ingredient is scaled by the recipe's
price multiplier, and can never be below zero.

special price =  This value is dynamically updated whenever a player starts and stops trading with a
villager that owns this merchant recipe. It is based on the player's individual reputation with the villager,
and the player's currently active status effects (ex: hero of the village).
The influence of the player's reputation on the special price is scaled by the recipe's price multiplier.

syntax_14960.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14960.title: ItemComponent - Container
syntax_14960.description:
Represents the container component of an item, providing access to its contents.
You can add items, remove items, delete the component, go nuts.
See [**Container Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#container) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
 - SET = Sets the container component to a specific set of items.
 - ADD = Adds items to the container component.
 - REMOVE = Removes items from the container component.
 - DELETE = Deletes the container component from the item.
 - RESET = Resets the container component to its default state.

en:
Represents the container component of an item, providing access to its contents.
You can add items, remove items, delete the component, go nuts.
See [**Container Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#container) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
 - SET = Sets the container component to a specific set of items.
 - ADD = Adds items to the container component.
 - REMOVE = Removes items from the container component.
 - DELETE = Deletes the container component from the item.
 - RESET = Resets the container component to its default state.

syntax_10793.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10793.title: VirtualFurnace - Furnace Item
syntax_10793.description: Get a virtual furnace item. This will be any item with a linked virtual furnace, allowing players to access a portable furnace wherever they go. The speed multipliers will allow your furnace item to cook faster or burn fuel faster. Ex: 1.0 will burn at normal speed as defined by the recipes, where as 2.0 will burn twice as fast. Omitting these values will default to 1.0.GUI name will be the name that shows up in the furnace GUI
syntax_10793.syntax_pattern:
[a] [(glowing)] virtual furnace item as %itemtype% with (inventory|gui) name %string% [[and ]with cook speed multiplier %number%] [[and ]with fuel speed multiplier %number%]
[a] [(glowing)] virtual furnace item as %itemtype% with (inventory|gui) name %string% 
with [[furnace ]properties] %-machineproperty%

en: Get a virtual furnace item. This will be any item with a linked virtual furnace, allowing players to access a portable furnace wherever they go. The speed multipliers will allow your furnace item to cook faster or burn fuel faster. Ex: 1.0 will burn at normal speed as defined by the recipes, where as 2.0 will burn twice as fast. Omitting these values will default to 1.0.GUI name will be the name that shows up in the furnace GUI

syntax_14961.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14961.title: ItemComponent - Stored Enchantments
syntax_14961.description: Get/set the stored enchantments of an enchanted book.

en: Get/set the stored enchantments of an enchanted book.

syntax_14962.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14962.title: Structure - Template BlockStates
syntax_14962.description:
Get a list of the blockstates in a structure template.
This represents the palette of blocks a structure template holds.

en:
Get a list of the blockstates in a structure template.
This represents the palette of blocks a structure template holds.

syntax_13173.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13173.title: ChunkGenerator - Biome Parameter Point Values
syntax_13173.description:
Represents the different values of a Biome Parameter Point.
This is used in the `biome gen` section of a chunk generator.
`fixed` = Will return the grouped ranged value of a param point, example for continentalness:
- `-1.2~-1.05` = 0 (Mushroom fields)
- `-1.05~-0.455` = 1 (Deep ocean)
- `-0.455~-0.19` = 2 (Ocean)
- `-0.19~-0.11` = 3 (Coast)
- `-0.11~0.03` = 4 (Near-inland)
- `0.03~0.3` = 5 (Mid-inland)
- `0.3~1.0` = 6 (Far-inland)
See [**World Generation/Biomes**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/World_generation#Biomes) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Represents the different values of a Biome Parameter Point.
This is used in the `biome gen` section of a chunk generator.
`fixed` = Will return the grouped ranged value of a param point, example for continentalness:
- `-1.2~-1.05` = 0 (Mushroom fields)
- `-1.05~-0.455` = 1 (Deep ocean)
- `-0.455~-0.19` = 2 (Ocean)
- `-0.19~-0.11` = 3 (Coast)
- `-0.11~0.03` = 4 (Near-inland)
- `0.03~0.3` = 5 (Mid-inland)
- `0.3~1.0` = 6 (Far-inland)
See [**World Generation/Biomes**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/World_generation#Biomes) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_14963.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14963.title: Structure - Template Entities
syntax_14963.description:
Get a list of entities in a structure template.
This cannot be modified.

en:
Get a list of entities in a structure template.
This cannot be modified.

syntax_14964.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14964.title: Structure - Template Last Placed Location
syntax_14964.description:
Represents the location of the last place a structure template was placed using the place structure effect.
**NOTE**: This will only be saved to file if you use the save effect after placing a structure,
otherwise it will not persist thru stop/restart.
syntax_14964.syntax_pattern:
[the] last placed location of %structuretemplates%
%structuretemplates%'[s] last placed location

en:
Represents the location of the last place a structure template was placed using the place structure effect.
**NOTE**: This will only be saved to file if you use the save effect after placing a structure,
otherwise it will not persist thru stop/restart.

syntax_14965.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14965.title: Structure - Template Object
syntax_14965.description:
Create a new, empty structure template or load a structure template from file.
If the file you have specified is not available, it will be created upon saving.
Structures without a namespace (ex: "tree") will default to the "minecraft" namespace and load from/save to "(main world folder)/generated/minecraft/structure/".
Structures with a namespace (ex:"myname:house") will load from/save to "(main world folder)/generated/myname/structure/".
To create folders, simply add a slash in your name, ex: "buildings/house".
Changes made to structures will not automatically be saved to file, you will need to use the save structure effect.
syntax_14965.syntax_pattern:
structure[s] (named|with id[s]|with key[s]) %strings%
structure template[s] (named|with id[s]|with key[s]) %strings%

en:
Create a new, empty structure template or load a structure template from file.
If the file you have specified is not available, it will be created upon saving.
Structures without a namespace (ex: "tree") will default to the "minecraft" namespace and load from/save to "(main world folder)/generated/minecraft/structure/".
Structures with a namespace (ex:"myname:house") will load from/save to "(main world folder)/generated/myname/structure/".
To create folders, simply add a slash in your name, ex: "buildings/house".
Changes made to structures will not automatically be saved to file, you will need to use the save structure effect.

syntax_14493.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14493.title: Bukkit Color
syntax_14493.description:
Creates a new Bukkit Color using alpha (transparency), red, green and blue channels.
Number values must be between 0 and 255.
syntax_14493.syntax_pattern: bukkitColor(alpha: number, red: number, green: number, blue: number)

en:
Creates a new Bukkit Color using alpha (transparency), red, green and blue channels.
Number values must be between 0 and 255.

syntax_11074.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11074.title: LootTable - Items
syntax_11074.description:
Get the items from a predefined LootTable.
Optionals:
Some loot tables will require some of these values whereas others may not.
`seed` = Represents the random seed used to generate loot (if not provided will generate randomly).
`looting modifier` = Set the looting enchant level equivalent to use when generating loot.
Values less than or equal to 0 will force the LootTable to only return a single Item per pool.
`luck` = How much luck to have when generating loot.
`killer/looter` = The Player that killed/looted. This Player will be used to get the looting level if looting modifier is not set.
`looted entity` = The Entity that was killed/looted.
syntax_11074.syntax_pattern: (looted|lootable) items from %loottable% [with seed %number%] [with looting modifier %number%] [with luck %number%] [with (killer|looter) %player%] [with looted entity %entity%]

en:
Get the items from a predefined LootTable.
Optionals:
Some loot tables will require some of these values whereas others may not.
`seed` = Represents the random seed used to generate loot (if not provided will generate randomly).
`looting modifier` = Set the looting enchant level equivalent to use when generating loot.
Values less than or equal to 0 will force the LootTable to only return a single Item per pool.
`luck` = How much luck to have when generating loot.
`killer/looter` = The Player that killed/looted. This Player will be used to get the looting level if looting modifier is not set.
`looted entity` = The Entity that was killed/looted.

syntax_14966.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14966.title: Structure - Template Properties
syntax_14966.description:
Represents different properties of a structure template, including mirroring, rotation, inclusion of entities and integrity.
These properties are only used for placing the structure template in a world, they are NOT saved to the structure file.
Mirror determines which way the structure mirrors, either 'none', 'front back' or 'left right'.
Rotation determines which way the structure is rotated, either 'none', 'clockwise 90', 'clockwise 180' or 'counterclockwise 90'.
Integrity determines how damaged the building should look by randomly skipping blocks to place. This value can range from 0 to 1.
With 0 removing all blocks and 1 spawning the structure in pristine condition.
Include entities determines if saved entities should be spawned into the structure template (true by default).
Size returns a vector offset from the starting point of the structure. This cannot be changed.

NOTE: `reset` will reset the value back to default. (added in v-2.7.2)
syntax_14966.syntax_pattern:
structure mirror of %structuretemplates%
structure rotation of %structuretemplates%
structure integrity of %structuretemplates%
structure include entities of %structuretemplates%
structure size of %structuretemplates%

en:
Represents different properties of a structure template, including mirroring, rotation, inclusion of entities and integrity.
These properties are only used for placing the structure template in a world, they are NOT saved to the structure file.
Mirror determines which way the structure mirrors, either 'none', 'front back' or 'left right'.
Rotation determines which way the structure is rotated, either 'none', 'clockwise 90', 'clockwise 180' or 'counterclockwise 90'.
Integrity determines how damaged the building should look by randomly skipping blocks to place. This value can range from 0 to 1.
With 0 removing all blocks and 1 spawning the structure in pristine condition.
Include entities determines if saved entities should be spawned into the structure template (true by default).
Size returns a vector offset from the starting point of the structure. This cannot be changed.

NOTE: `reset` will reset the value back to default. (added in v-2.7.2)

syntax_11568.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11568.title: ChunkGenerator - ChunkData Block
syntax_11568.description:
Represents blocks in a ChunkData.
The first pattern is used to set a block in a `chunk gen` or `block pop` section.
The second pattern is used to fill blocks between 2 points in a `chunk gen` section.
NOTE: The vector represents the position in a chunk, not a world.
NOTE: You CAN reach into neighbouring chunks going below 0/above 15 in the vector. I don't know how far you can safely reach though.
syntax_11568.syntax_pattern:
chunk[ ]data block[data] at %vector%
chunk[ ]data block[data] at %number%, %number%, %number%
chunk[ ]data block[data]s (between|within) %vector% (and|to) %vector%
chunk[ ]data block[data]s (between|within) %number%, %number%, %number% (and|to) %number%, %number%, %number%

en:
Represents blocks in a ChunkData.
The first pattern is used to set a block in a `chunk gen` or `block pop` section.
The second pattern is used to fill blocks between 2 points in a `chunk gen` section.
NOTE: The vector represents the position in a chunk, not a world.
NOTE: You CAN reach into neighbouring chunks going below 0/above 15 in the vector. I don't know how far you can safely reach though.

syntax_14967.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14967.title: Structure - Template
syntax_14967.description:
Represents a structure template that can be saved or pasted into a world.
See [**Structure Templates**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Structure_file) on McWiki for more info.
These can also be placed using the `/place template` command.
Use the 'Structure-Object' expression to get a new/existing structure object.
When using `all structures`, this will only show structures that have been currently loaded into the game.

en:
Represents a structure template that can be saved or pasted into a world.
See [**Structure Templates**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Structure_file) on McWiki for more info.
These can also be placed using the `/place template` command.
Use the 'Structure-Object' expression to get a new/existing structure object.
When using `all structures`, this will only show structures that have been currently loaded into the game.

Abort a creature spawn event, preventing it from retrying more attempts after canceling.
Aborting should only be done if you're blanket blocking this entity type from spawning.

syntax_10508.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10508.title: Advancement - Progress Criteria
syntax_10508.description: Award or revoke criteria of an advancement progress.

en: Award or revoke criteria of an advancement progress.

syntax_10489.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10489.title: Is Critical Hit
syntax_10489.description: Check if a hit was a critical hit in a damage event. Requires a PaperMC server.

en: Check if a hit was a critical hit in a damage event. Requires a PaperMC server.

syntax_10507.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10507.title: Advancement - Load
syntax_10507.description:
Load an advancement represented by the specified string into the server.
The advancement format is governed by Minecraft.
It is currently a JSON object, as described by [**Advancement Definition**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Advancement_definition) on McWiki.
NOTE: Bukkit has marked this as 'Unsafe', so please use at your own risk.
Watch console for errors when loading an advancement.

en:
Load an advancement represented by the specified string into the server.
The advancement format is governed by Minecraft.
It is currently a JSON object, as described by [**Advancement Definition**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Advancement_definition) on McWiki.
NOTE: Bukkit has marked this as 'Unsafe', so please use at your own risk.
Watch console for errors when loading an advancement.

syntax_10509.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10509.title: Blockstate - Update/Apply
syntax_10509.description:
Attempts to update the block represented by this state.
Unless force is applied, this will not modify the state of a block if it is no longer
the same type as it was when this state was taken.
You can optionally apply this blockstate to another block.
If done without physics updates, it will not trigger a physics update on surrounding blocks which
could cause them to update or disappear.

en:
Attempts to update the block represented by this state.
Unless force is applied, this will not modify the state of a block if it is no longer
the same type as it was when this state was taken.
You can optionally apply this blockstate to another block.
If done without physics updates, it will not trigger a physics update on surrounding blocks which
could cause them to update or disappear.

syntax_10527.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10527.title: Open Container Animation
syntax_10527.description:
Play the open/close animation on a lidded block (ie: chest, barrel or shulker box).
Note: When using the open method, the block will basically be locked 'open', a player opening/closing the block will not close the lid.

en:
Play the open/close animation on a lidded block (ie: chest, barrel or shulker box).
Note: When using the open method, the block will basically be locked 'open', a player opening/closing the block will not close the lid.

syntax_10517.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10517.title: EntityBlockStorage - Add/Release Entities
syntax_10517.description:
Add/release entities to/from blocks which can store entities.
When releasing bees at night/during rain, they will immediately go back in their hive, use the optional timespan to keep them outside.
You can optionally put the released entities into a variable (see examples).
As of 1.15 this only includes beehives/bee nests!
syntax_10517.syntax_pattern:
release [all] entities from [storage of] %blocks% [for %-timespan%] [into %-objects%]
add %entities% to storage of %block%

en:
Add/release entities to/from blocks which can store entities.
When releasing bees at night/during rain, they will immediately go back in their hive, use the optional timespan to keep them outside.
You can optionally put the released entities into a variable (see examples).
As of 1.15 this only includes beehives/bee nests!

syntax_10520.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10520.title: Hurt Animation
syntax_10520.description:
Makes this living entity flash red as if they were damaged. Requires MC 1.20.1+

Note: Yaw = The direction the damage is coming from in relation to the entity,
where 0 is in front of the player, 90 is to the right, 180 is behind, and 270 is to the left

en:
Makes this living entity flash red as if they were damaged. Requires MC 1.20.1+

Note: Yaw = The direction the damage is coming from in relation to the entity,
where 0 is in front of the player, 90 is to the right, 180 is behind, and 270 is to the left

syntax_10521.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10521.title: List/Unlist Players
syntax_10521.description: List/unlist a player for a player. (show/hide them in PlayerList for each other).

en: List/unlist a player for a player. (show/hide them in PlayerList for each other).

syntax_10514.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10514.title: Drop Held Item
syntax_10514.description:
Forces the player to drop their currently held item.
By default it will drop one of their held item, or you can optionally drop the whole stack.

en:
Forces the player to drop their currently held item.
By default it will drop one of their held item, or you can optionally drop the whole stack.

syntax_10512.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10512.title: DamageSource - Damage Entity
syntax_10512.description:
Damage entities using a damage source.
This has the same functionality as Minecraft's `/damage` command.
Requires MC 1.20.4+

en:
Damage entities using a damage source.
This has the same functionality as Minecraft's `/damage` command.
Requires MC 1.20.4+

syntax_10518.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10518.title: Fish Hook - Pull In
syntax_10518.description: Pulls in the entity hooked to this fish hook.

en: Pulls in the entity hooked to this fish hook.

syntax_10524.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10524.title: NBT - Save File
syntax_10524.description:
Manually save the NBT from a file. When getting the NBT compound from a file, changing values in the compound will
not be automatically applied to the file, and saving will have to be done manually.

en:
Manually save the NBT from a file. When getting the NBT compound from a file, changing values in the compound will
not be automatically applied to the file, and saving will have to be done manually.

syntax_10519.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10519.title: Give or Drop Item
syntax_10519.description: Attempts to give an item to a player and if they dont have room it will drop instead.

en: Attempts to give an item to a player and if they dont have room it will drop instead.

syntax_10523.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10523.title: Merchant - Open
syntax_10523.description: Open a merchant/villager to a player.

en: Open a merchant/villager to a player.

syntax_10525.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10525.title: NBT - Set Block with NBT
syntax_10525.description: Set a block at a location to a block with NBT. BlockData is also supported when using MC 1.13+ and Skript 2.5+

en: Set a block at a location to a block with NBT. BlockData is also supported when using MC 1.13+ and Skript 2.5+

syntax_10526.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10526.title: NBT - Spawn Entity with NBT
syntax_10526.description:
Spawn an entity at a location with NBT.
The ability to spawn falling was added in 2.10.0 as a temp effect until Skript properly handles falling blocks
with block data.
syntax_10526.syntax_pattern:
spawn %entitytypes% [%directions% %locations%] with [nbt] %nbtcompound%
spawn %number% of %entitytypes% [%directions% %locations%] with [nbt] %nbtcompound%
spawn falling %blockdata% [%directions% %locations%] with [nbt] %nbtcompound%

en:
Spawn an entity at a location with NBT.
The ability to spawn falling was added in 2.10.0 as a temp effect until Skript properly handles falling blocks
with block data.

syntax_10536.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10536.title: Recipe - Knowledge Book
syntax_10536.description:
Add/Remove custom or minecraft recipes to/from a knowledge book item.
Optional string for plugin name, to add recipes from other plugins.
syntax_10536.syntax_pattern:
add [(custom|(mc|minecraft))] recipe[s] [with id[s]] %strings% [from plugin %-string%] to %itemtype%
remove [(custom|(mc|minecraft))] recipe[s] [with id[s]] %strings% [from plugin %-string%] from %itemtype%

en:
Add/Remove custom or minecraft recipes to/from a knowledge book item.
Optional string for plugin name, to add recipes from other plugins.

syntax_10529.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10529.title: Parse Effect
syntax_10529.description:
This will parse a string as an effect, and then executes it
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

en:
This will parse a string as an effect, and then executes it
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

syntax_10532.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10532.title: Ranged Attack
syntax_10532.description:
Make a RangedEntity perform a ranged attack. Requires PaperMC.

`with charge` = How "charged" the attack is (how far back the bow was pulled for Bow attacks).
This should be a value between 0 and 1, represented as targetDistance/maxDistance.

RangedEntities: Drowned, Illusioner, Llama, Piglin, Pillager, Skeleton, Snowman, Stray, TraderLlama, Witch, Wither, WitherSkeleton.

en:
Make a RangedEntity perform a ranged attack. Requires PaperMC.

`with charge` = How "charged" the attack is (how far back the bow was pulled for Bow attacks).
This should be a value between 0 and 1, represented as targetDistance/maxDistance.

RangedEntities: Drowned, Illusioner, Llama, Piglin, Pillager, Skeleton, Snowman, Stray, TraderLlama, Witch, Wither, WitherSkeleton.

syntax_10537.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10537.title: Recipe - Remove
syntax_10537.description:
Remove a recipe from your server. Recipes can be removed at any time 
but it is best to do so during a server load event. If a recipe is removed whilst a player is online 
it will still show up in their recipe book, but they will not be able to craft it. If need be, you can get 
a list of all recipes by simply typing "/minecraft:recipe give YourName " in game.
You can remove Minecraft recipes, custom recipes and recipes from other plugins.

en:
Remove a recipe from your server. Recipes can be removed at any time 
but it is best to do so during a server load event. If a recipe is removed whilst a player is online 
it will still show up in their recipe book, but they will not be able to craft it. If need be, you can get 
a list of all recipes by simply typing "/minecraft:recipe give YourName " in game.
You can remove Minecraft recipes, custom recipes and recipes from other plugins.

syntax_10558.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10558.title: TextComponent - Sign Change
syntax_10558.description:
Sends a sign change to the player. You CAN send a block change first.
Side = Whether to show the front/back change (defaults to front).
Color = the color of the text on the sign.
Glowing = make the text glow.
syntax_10558.syntax_pattern: make %players% see [front|back] sign lines (at|of) %blocks% as %textcomps% [with color %-color%] [and glowing]

en:
Sends a sign change to the player. You CAN send a block change first.
Side = Whether to show the front/back change (defaults to front).
Color = the color of the text on the sign.
Glowing = make the text glow.

syntax_10534.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10534.title: Recipe - Discovery
syntax_10534.description: Lock/Unlock recipes for players. This uses the IDs we created earlier when registering recipes, you can also lock/unlock minecraft recipes.
syntax_10534.syntax_pattern:
(discover|unlock) [(custom|mc|minecraft)] recipe[s] [with id[s]] %strings% for %players%
(undiscover|lock) [(custom|mc|minecraft)] recipe[s] [with id[s]] %strings% for %players%

en: Lock/Unlock recipes for players. This uses the IDs we created earlier when registering recipes, you can also lock/unlock minecraft recipes.

syntax_10535.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10535.title: Recipe - Ingredient
syntax_10535.description:
Set/add to the ingredients of a shaped/shapeless recipe.
This is specifically used in the ingredients section of shaped/shapeless recipe section.
`set ingredient` is used for shaped recipes.
`add ingredient` is used for shapeless recipes.
syntax_10535.syntax_pattern:
set ingredient (of|for) %string% to %itemstack/recipechoice/minecrafttag%
add %itemstack/recipechoice/minecrafttag% to ingredients

en:
Set/add to the ingredients of a shaped/shapeless recipe.
This is specifically used in the ingredients section of shaped/shapeless recipe section.
`set ingredient` is used for shaped recipes.
`add ingredient` is used for shapeless recipes.

syntax_10528.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10528.title: Open Sign
syntax_10528.description:
Open a sign's GUI to a player, to allow them to edit it.
`virtual`: (Added in Paper 1.21.5)
 - Allows opening a client side sign to a player.
 - You will have to send a client side sign to the player.
 - The sign must be within 9 blocks of the player.
 - You can use the `unchecked sign change` event to listen for changes to virtual signs.
If not using `virtual`, the block must be placed in the world.
Front/Back support added in MC 1.20.
syntax_10528.syntax_pattern:
open [virtual] sign [gui] [(front|back)] [(for|of)] [%direction%] %location% to %players%
open [%direction%] %location%'[s] [virtual] sign [gui] [(front|back)] to %players%

en:
Open a sign's GUI to a player, to allow them to edit it.
`virtual`: (Added in Paper 1.21.5)
 - Allows opening a client side sign to a player.
 - You will have to send a client side sign to the player.
 - The sign must be within 9 blocks of the player.
 - You can use the `unchecked sign change` event to listen for changes to virtual signs.
If not using `virtual`, the block must be placed in the world.
Front/Back support added in MC 1.20.

syntax_10531.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10531.title: Random Tick Block
syntax_10531.description:
Causes the block to be ticked randomly.
This will tick the block the same way Minecraft randomly ticks according to the randomTickSpeed gamerule.
Requires Paper 1.19+

en:
Causes the block to be ticked randomly.
This will tick the block the same way Minecraft randomly ticks according to the randomTickSpeed gamerule.
Requires Paper 1.19+

syntax_10847.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10847.title: Scoreboard - Objective Render Type
syntax_10847.description: Controls the way in which an Objective is rendered client side.

en: Controls the way in which an Objective is rendered client side.

syntax_10543.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10543.title: Send Block Damage
syntax_10543.description:
Send fake block damage to a player.

Number = The amount of damage (a number between 0 and 1) to be applied to the block.

Entity = the entity who is damaging the block. An entity can only damage 1 block at a time.

By Entity/Number = The entity/entityID which damaged the block.

en:
Send fake block damage to a player.

Number = The amount of damage (a number between 0 and 1) to be applied to the block.

Entity = the entity who is damaging the block. An entity can only damage 1 block at a time.

By Entity/Number = The entity/entityID which damaged the block.

syntax_10549.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10549.title: Spawner - Reset Timer
syntax_10549.description: Resets the spawner timer for a spawner.

en: Resets the spawner timer for a spawner.

syntax_10540.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10540.title: Recipe - StoneCutting Recipe
syntax_10540.description:
Register a new stone cutting recipe.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book.
syntax_10540.syntax_pattern: register [new] stone[ ]cutt(ing|er) recipe for %itemtype% (using|with ingredient) %itemtype/recipechoice% with id %string% [in group %-string%]

en:
Register a new stone cutting recipe.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
You may also include an optional group for recipes. These will group the recipes together in the recipe book.

syntax_10552.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10552.title: Structure - Save/Delete
syntax_10552.description: Save a structure template to file (will overwrite if file already exists), or delete a structure template file.

en: Save a structure template to file (will overwrite if file already exists), or delete a structure template file.

syntax_10848.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10848.title: Spellcaster Spell
syntax_10848.description:
Represents the different spells of a spellcaster.
NOTE: These are auto-generated and may differ between server versions, currently generated for Minecraft 26.1.2.
syntax_10848.type_usage: blindness, disappear, fangs, none, summon_vex, wololo

en:
Represents the different spells of a spellcaster.
NOTE: These are auto-generated and may differ between server versions, currently generated for Minecraft 26.1.2.

syntax_10544.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10544.title: Server Tick - Sprint
syntax_10544.description:
Attempts to initiate a sprint, which executes all server ticks at a faster rate then normal.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Attempts to initiate a sprint, which executes all server ticks at a faster rate then normal.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10547.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10547.title: Show/Hide Entities
syntax_10547.description:
Show/hide entities to/from players.
NOTE: This is not persistent across server restarts and/or chunk unloading!

en:
Show/hide entities to/from players.
NOTE: This is not persistent across server restarts and/or chunk unloading!

syntax_10557.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10557.title: TextComponent - Send Title
syntax_10557.description:
Send titles containing components. Supports strings as well.
If you are using variables and the title won't send, make sure to add `component`.
`to %audiences%` = An audience is anything that can receieve a component (players, entities, console, worlds, server, etc).

en:
Send titles containing components. Supports strings as well.
If you are using variables and the title won't send, make sure to add `component`.
`to %audiences%` = An audience is anything that can receieve a component (players, entities, console, worlds, server, etc).

syntax_10546.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10546.title: Show Demo Screen
syntax_10546.description:
Shows the demo screen to the player, this screen is normally only seen in the demo version of the game.
Servers can modify the text on this screen using a resource pack.

en:
Shows the demo screen to the player, this screen is normally only seen in the demo version of the game.
Servers can modify the text on this screen using a resource pack.

syntax_10548.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10548.title: Spawn Falling BlockData
syntax_10548.description:
Spawn a falling block data.
This is a temp effect until Skript properly handles block data with spawning of falling blocks.

en:
Spawn a falling block data.
This is a temp effect until Skript properly handles block data with spawning of falling blocks.

syntax_10553.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10553.title: Task - Cancel Task
syntax_10553.description:
Stop a task by ID.
If running Folia, this effect will do nothing.

en:
Stop a task by ID.
If running Folia, this effect will do nothing.

syntax_10561.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10561.title: VirtualFurnace - Furnace Fuel
syntax_10561.description: Register recipes for fuels for virtual furnaces. Burn time will determine how long this fuel will burn for.Alternatively you can use the all vanilla fuel effect, and it will register all fuels that match vanilla fuels.Due to a limitation in Minecraft, this only accepts items which can be used as fuels in vanilla furnaces.
syntax_10561.syntax_pattern:
register furnace fuel %itemtype% with burn time %timespan%
register all vanilla [furnace] fuels

en: Register recipes for fuels for virtual furnaces. Burn time will determine how long this fuel will burn for.Alternatively you can use the all vanilla fuel effect, and it will register all fuels that match vanilla fuels.Due to a limitation in Minecraft, this only accepts items which can be used as fuels in vanilla furnaces.

syntax_10572.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10572.title: ArmorTrim - Material/Pattern
syntax_10572.description:
Represents the material and pattern of an armor trim.
These cannot be changed, you'd have to create a new armor trim.

en:
Represents the material and pattern of an armor trim.
These cannot be changed, you'd have to create a new armor trim.

syntax_10570.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10570.title: ArmorTrim - Create
syntax_10570.description: Create a new armor trim to be applied to an item.

en: Create a new armor trim to be applied to an item.

syntax_10568.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10568.title: Advancement - Progress Criteria
syntax_10568.description: Get the awarded/remaining criteria of an advancement progress.

en: Get the awarded/remaining criteria of an advancement progress.

syntax_10562.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10562.title: VirtualFurnace - Furnace Recipe
syntax_10562.description:
Register recipes for virtual furnaces.
Alternatively you can just register all vanilla-like recipes for your virtual furnaces.
syntax_10562.syntax_pattern:
register virtual furnace recipe for %itemtype% using %itemtype% [with cooktime %-timespan%]
register all virtual (furnace|smoker|blast furnace) recipes

en:
Register recipes for virtual furnaces.
Alternatively you can just register all vanilla-like recipes for your virtual furnaces.

syntax_10569.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10569.title: Armor Change Event - Item
syntax_10569.description: Get the old/new item in an armor change event. Defaults to new. When using set, it will set the slot that was changed, old/new will make no difference in this case. Requires Paper 1.12.2+

en: Get the old/new item in an armor change event. Defaults to new. When using set, it will set the slot that was changed, old/new will make no difference in this case. Requires Paper 1.12.2+

syntax_10575.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10575.title: Beacon - Entities in Effect Range
syntax_10575.description: Returns a list of entities in the effect range of a beacon.

en: Returns a list of entities in the effect range of a beacon.

syntax_10576.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10576.title: Beacon - Primary/Secondary Effect
syntax_10576.description:
The primary/secondary potion effect of a beacon.

Note:

When getting the primary/secondary potion effect it will return as a Potion Effect and not a Potion Effect Type.

If you update the beacon inventory by opening it and placing an item you will lose any custom effects not normally applicable.

en:
The primary/secondary potion effect of a beacon.

Note:

When getting the primary/secondary potion effect it will return as a Potion Effect and not a Potion Effect Type.

If you update the beacon inventory by opening it and placing an item you will lose any custom effects not normally applicable.

syntax_10560.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10560.title: Villager - Effects
syntax_10560.description: A few effects to make villagers do things.
syntax_10560.syntax_pattern:
zombify %livingentities%
wake[ ]up %livingentities%
make %livingentities% shake [(his|their)] head[s]
make %livingentities% sleep at %location%

en: A few effects to make villagers do things.

syntax_10578.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10578.title: Block - Exact Location
syntax_10578.description:
Returns the EXACT location of a block.
Skript's `location of block` adds 0.5 to each x,y,z coord of the location, the "center" of the block.
This expression will return the true location of a block.

en:
Returns the EXACT location of a block.
Skript's `location of block` adds 0.5 to each x,y,z coord of the location, the "center" of the block.
This expression will return the true location of a block.

syntax_10565.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10565.title: Advancement - All Available
syntax_10565.description: Get a list of all available advancements currently registered on the server.

en: Get a list of all available advancements currently registered on the server.

syntax_10577.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10577.title: Beacon - Tier
syntax_10577.description: Returns the current tier of a beacon block.

en: Returns the current tier of a beacon block.

syntax_10579.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10579.title: Block - Relative
syntax_10579.description: Get a block relative to another block using a BlockFace.

en: Get a block relative to another block using a BlockFace.

syntax_10574.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10574.title: Beacon - Effect Range
syntax_10574.description:
Gets the effect range of a beacon.
A negative range value means the beacon is using its default range based on tier.

en:
Gets the effect range of a beacon.
A negative range value means the beacon is using its default range based on tier.

syntax_10564.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10564.title: Absorption Amount
syntax_10564.description: Represents the absorption amount of an entity.

en: Represents the absorption amount of an entity.

syntax_10566.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10566.title: Advancement - Criteria
syntax_10566.description: Get a list of the criteria for an advancement.

en: Get a list of the criteria for an advancement.

syntax_10599.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10599.title: Bound - Bounds at Location
syntax_10599.description: Get a list of non-temporary, temporary, or all bounds bounds/ids at a location.

en: Get a list of non-temporary, temporary, or all bounds bounds/ids at a location.

syntax_10584.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10584.title: BlockData - Tags
syntax_10584.description: Get a list of all block data tags of a Block or BlockData.

en: Get a list of all block data tags of a Block or BlockData.

syntax_10591.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10591.title: Book - Pages
syntax_10591.description: Allows you to set pages in a book to text components. You can also retrieve the pages. Based on testing, a book's author/title needs to be set AFTER setting the pages, why? I have no idea!

en: Allows you to set pages in a book to text components. You can also retrieve the pages. Based on testing, a book's author/title needs to be set AFTER setting the pages, why? I have no idea!

syntax_10583.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10583.title: BlockData - Tag
syntax_10583.description: Get/set a block data tag of BlockData or a Block.

en: Get/set a block data tag of BlockData or a Block.

syntax_10587.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10587.title: BlockState - BlockData
syntax_10587.description: Represents the blockdata of a block state.

en: Represents the blockdata of a block state.

syntax_10582.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10582.title: Block in Chunk
syntax_10582.description: Represents a block in a chunk. X/Z coords will be a value from 0 to 15.

en: Represents a block in a chunk. X/Z coords will be a value from 0 to 15.

syntax_10594.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10594.title: BossBar - Entity/Player
syntax_10594.description:
Get a BossBar from an entity (such as a wither) or all BossBars of players.
NOTE: BossBars from entities cannot be saved in global variables, as the entity may not be loaded on the
server when that variable is trying to load. Custom BossBars and BossBars from players can be saved in variables.

en:
Get a BossBar from an entity (such as a wither) or all BossBars of players.
NOTE: BossBars from entities cannot be saved in global variables, as the entity may not be loaded on the
server when that variable is trying to load. Custom BossBars and BossBars from players can be saved in variables.

syntax_10600.creator: ShaneBee

[(all [[of] the]|the)] bound blocks within %bound%
[(all [[of] the]|the)] blocks within bound %bound%

syntax_10601.creator: ShaneBee

syntax_10597.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10597.title: Bound - All Bounds

syntax_10595.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10595.title: BossBar - From ID

en: Get an already created BossBar from ID (this will NOT create a new one).

syntax_10604.creator: eyesniper2

syntax_10596.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10596.title: BossBar - Properties
syntax_10596.description:
Represents the properties of a BossBar that can be changed.
Progress of a bar is a number from 0-100.
syntax_10596.syntax_pattern:
[boss[ ]]bar players of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar (color|colour) of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar style of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar title of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar progress of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar flag %bossbarflag% of %bossbar%
[boss[ ]]bar visibility of %bossbar%

en:
Represents the properties of a BossBar that can be changed.
Progress of a bar is a number from 0-100.

syntax_10590.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10590.title: BlockState - Offset
syntax_10590.description:
Represents the offset of a blockstate in a structure.
This is a vector, distance from the starting block of a structure.

en:
Represents the offset of a blockstate in a structure.
This is a vector, distance from the starting block of a structure.

syntax_10589.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10589.title: BlockState - ItemType

en: Represents the itemtype of a block state.

syntax_10592.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10592.title: BossBar - All

en: Get a list of all BossBars.

syntax_10581.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10581.title: Block Dropped Items
syntax_10581.description:
Represents the dropped items in a block drop item event.
`block dropped items` = The dropped item entities.
`block dropped itemtypes` = The dropped item.
syntax_10581.syntax_pattern:
block dropped (items|item entities)
block dropped itemtypes

en:
Represents the dropped items in a block drop item event.
`block dropped items` = The dropped item entities.
`block dropped itemtypes` = The dropped item.

syntax_10585.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10585.title: BlockData - Updates

en: Set the BlockData of a block without updates (will prevent physics updates of neighbouring blocks).

syntax_10586.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10586.title: BlockState - Block
syntax_10586.description: Gets the block represented by this block state. Will return nothing if blockstate is not placed in the world.

en: Gets the block represented by this block state. Will return nothing if blockstate is not placed in the world.

syntax_10614.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10614.title: Chunk at Coords
syntax_10614.description:
Get a chunk using chunk coords.
NOTE: Chunk coords are different than location coords.
Chunk coords are basically location coords divided by 16.
Optionally get the chunk without generating it (possibly doesn't load as well).

en:
Get a chunk using chunk coords.
NOTE: Chunk coords are different than location coords.
Chunk coords are basically location coords divided by 16.
Optionally get the chunk without generating it (possibly doesn't load as well).

syntax_10618.creator: eyesniper2

[the] (causing entity|direct entity|damage type|damage location|food exhaustion|source location) of %damagesources%
%damagesources%'[s] (causing entity|direct entity|damage type|damage location|food exhaustion|source location)

en: Represents different elements you can get from a damage source.

syntax_10616.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10616.title: DamageSource - Create
syntax_10616.description:
Create a new damage source which includes a damage type and some optional values.
Optional Values:
`caused by %entity%` = The entity that caused the damage.
`directly by %entity%` = The entity that directly inflicted the damage.
`at %location%` = The source of the damage.

en:
Create a new damage source which includes a damage type and some optional values.
Optional Values:
`caused by %entity%` = The entity that caused the damage.
`directly by %entity%` = The entity that directly inflicted the damage.
`at %location%` = The source of the damage.

syntax_10463.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10463.title: Player Elytra Boost
syntax_10463.description:
Fired when a player boosts elytra flight with a firework.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-item type`
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-item stack`
 - `event-entity`
syntax_10463.event_values: event-entity, event-item stack, event-item type, event-player, event-world

en:
Fired when a player boosts elytra flight with a firework.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-item type`
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-item stack`
 - `event-entity`

syntax_10615.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10615.title: Client Brand
syntax_10615.description:
Returns player's client brand name. If the client didn't send this information, the brand name will be null.
For the Notchian client this name defaults to "vanilla". Some modified clients report other names such as "forge".
Requires a PaperMC server.

en:
Returns player's client brand name. If the client didn't send this information, the brand name will be null.
For the Notchian client this name defaults to "vanilla". Some modified clients report other names such as "forge".
Requires a PaperMC server.
Returns the last created bound.

syntax_10605.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10605.title: Bound - ID
syntax_10605.description:
Get/set the id of a bound. When setting the ID of a bound, if another bound has that ID, this will fail with an error in console.
You cannot set the IDs of multiple bounds at once.

en:
Get/set the id of a bound. When setting the ID of a bound, if another bound has that ID, this will fail with an error in console.
You cannot set the IDs of multiple bounds at once.

syntax_10610.creator: eyesniper2

syntax_10610.description:

Setting requires a PaperMC server.
RangedEntities: Drowned, Illusioner, Llama, Piglin, Pillager, Skeleton, Snowman, Stray, TraderLlama, Witch, Wither, WitherSkeleton.
