Represents a RangedEntity that is "charging" up an attack, by raising its hands.
Setting requires a PaperMC server.
RangedEntities: Drowned, Illusioner, Llama, Piglin, Pillager, Skeleton, Snowman, Stray, TraderLlama, Witch, Wither, WitherSkeleton.

Removed in Minecraft 1.21.2.
See 'Apply Lock to Block' effect to apply an item as a lock to a block.

Get or set the lock of a container or beacon.
Removed in Minecraft 1.21.2.
See 'Apply Lock to Block' effect to apply an item as a lock to a block.

syntax_10682.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10682.title: Merchant Recipe - Ingredients
syntax_10682.description:
Represents the ingredients/result of a merchant recipe.
Ingredients can be set, but the result can not be changed.
If you wish to change the result, you will have to create a new merchant recipe.

en:
Represents the ingredients/result of a merchant recipe.
Ingredients can be set, but the result can not be changed.
If you wish to change the result, you will have to create a new merchant recipe.

syntax_10673.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10673.title: Interaction - Last Attack/Interaction Player
syntax_10673.description:
Represents the last player to attack/interact with an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Represents the last player to attack/interact with an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10674.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10674.title: Interaction - Last Attack/Interaction Timestamp
syntax_10674.description:
Represents the timestamp of the last attack/interaction of an Interaction Entity.
As per Minecraft "The timestamp of the game tick when the event happened".
Appears to be how many ticks since the world was created.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Represents the timestamp of the last attack/interaction of an Interaction Entity.
As per Minecraft "The timestamp of the game tick when the event happened".
Appears to be how many ticks since the world was created.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10683.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10683.title: Min/Max World Height
syntax_10683.description: Get the min/max height of a world.

en: Get the min/max height of a world.

syntax_10686.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10686.title: Minecraft Tag - Get
syntax_10686.description: Get a Minecraft Tag. This will include vanilla MC tags and custom tags from data packs that are registered to the server.

en: Get a Minecraft Tag. This will include vanilla MC tags and custom tags from data packs that are registered to the server.

syntax_10705.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10705.title: Player Idle Time
syntax_10705.description:
Get/reset the idle time of a player.
The idle duration is reset when the player sends specific action packets.
Requires Paper 1.20.2+

en:
Get/reset the idle time of a player.
The idle duration is reset when the player sends specific action packets.
Requires Paper 1.20.2+

syntax_10687.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10687.title: Minecraft Tag - Values
syntax_10687.description: Get the values of a Minecraft Tag.

en: Get the values of a Minecraft Tag.

syntax_10671.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10671.title: Interaction - Interaction Width/Height
syntax_10671.description:
Represents the interaction width/height of an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Represents the interaction width/height of an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10672.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10672.title: Interaction - Is Responsive
syntax_10672.description:
Represents the state of being responsive of an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Represents the state of being responsive of an Interaction Entity.
See [**Interaction Entity Data**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Interaction#Entity_data) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10684.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10684.title: Minecraft Tag - All
syntax_10684.description: Represents all Minecraft Tags registered to the server.

en: Represents all Minecraft Tags registered to the server.

syntax_10685.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10685.title: Minecraft Tag - Custom Tag
syntax_10685.description: Create your own custom tag with items of your choosing. Requires a PaperMC server.
syntax_10685.syntax_pattern: custom minecraft tag with (key|id) %namespacedkey/string% to include %itemtypes%

en: Create your own custom tag with items of your choosing. Requires a PaperMC server.

syntax_10692.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10692.title: NBT - Item with NBT
syntax_10692.description:
Get an item with nbt.
NOTE: The NBT in the examples represents NBT for Minecraft 1.20.5+
NOTE: The optional `custom` syntax will place nbt in the `"minecraft:custom_data"` component (1.20.5+ only).

en:
Get an item with nbt.
NOTE: The NBT in the examples represents NBT for Minecraft 1.20.5+
NOTE: The optional `custom` syntax will place nbt in the `"minecraft:custom_data"` component (1.20.5+ only).

syntax_10701.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10701.title: Parse Effect - With Return
syntax_10701.description:
This will parse a string as an effect and then execute it and returns whether or not it executed.
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

en:
This will parse a string as an effect and then execute it and returns whether or not it executed.
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

syntax_10703.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10703.title: Pathfinding - Path Target
syntax_10703.description:
Set the path of an entity to target a specific location, with an optional speed
Get the location of the paths end. Delete will stop the entity from pathfinding. Requires Paper 1.13+
syntax_10703.syntax_pattern:
[the] [final] path target[s] [with speed %-number%] of %livingentities%
%livingentities%'[s] [final] path target[s] [with speed %-number%]

en:
Set the path of an entity to target a specific location, with an optional speed
Get the location of the paths end. Delete will stop the entity from pathfinding. Requires Paper 1.13+

syntax_10697.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10697.title: NBT - UUID for NBT
syntax_10697.description:
Allows you to get an entity's UUID which can be represented in an NBT compound. Prior to 1.16 UUIDs were represented 
as most/least significant bits. As of 1.16, they are now represented as int arrays. If the player/entity is excluded, this 
will return a random UUID.
syntax_10697.syntax_pattern: uuid (int array[( as string)]|most[ bits]|least[ bits]) [(from|of) %-offlineplayer/entity%]

en:
Allows you to get an entity's UUID which can be represented in an NBT compound. Prior to 1.16 UUIDs were represented 
as most/least significant bits. As of 1.16, they are now represented as int arrays. If the player/entity is excluded, this 
will return a random UUID.

syntax_10693.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10693.title: NBT - Pretty NBT String
syntax_10693.description:
Get a 'pretty' NBT string. This is colored the same as when using the vanilla Minecraft '/data' command. 
Splitting it will output kind of like a JSON output. Requires 1.13.2+
syntax_10693.syntax_pattern:
pretty nbt (of|from) %nbtcompounds% [(with|using) split %-string%]
%nbtcompounds%'[s] pretty nbt [(with|using) split %-string%]

en:
Get a 'pretty' NBT string. This is colored the same as when using the vanilla Minecraft '/data' command. 
Splitting it will output kind of like a JSON output. Requires 1.13.2+

syntax_10699.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10699.title: Nearest Entity
syntax_10699.description:
Returns the nearest entity around a location/entity. Requires PaperMC.

NOTE: When using `around entity`, this will exclude that entity in the search.

NOTE: When radius is excluded, the distance will default to Skript's `maximum target block distance`.

en:
Returns the nearest entity around a location/entity. Requires PaperMC.

NOTE: When using `around entity`, this will exclude that entity in the search.

NOTE: When radius is excluded, the distance will default to Skript's `maximum target block distance`.

syntax_10696.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10696.title: NBT - Tags
syntax_10696.description: Get all tags of an NBT compound.

en: Get all tags of an NBT compound.

syntax_10695.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10695.title: NBT - Tag Type
syntax_10695.description: Represents the NBT tag type of a tag in an NBT compound. This is mainly useful for debugging purposes.

en: Represents the NBT tag type of a tag in an NBT compound. This is mainly useful for debugging purposes.

syntax_10702.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10702.title: Pathfinding - Path Points
syntax_10702.description: Get all the points along an entity's pathfinding path. Requires Paper 1.13+

en: Get all the points along an entity's pathfinding path. Requires Paper 1.13+

syntax_10700.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10700.title: NamespacedKey - Object From
syntax_10700.description:
Get an object from a namespaced key.
This may come in handy in an instance you have a string version that doesn't match Skript and can't be parsed.
Also supports some custom objects such as custom Enchantments in Minecraft 1.21+
Currently supported types: attribute, biome, damage type, enchantment, entity type, game event, item type,
particle, potion effect type, statistic, world.

en:
Get an object from a namespaced key.
This may come in handy in an instance you have a string version that doesn't match Skript and can't be parsed.
Also supports some custom objects such as custom Enchantments in Minecraft 1.21+
Currently supported types: attribute, biome, damage type, enchantment, entity type, game event, item type,
particle, potion effect type, statistic, world.

syntax_10691.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10691.title: NBT - Item from NBT
syntax_10691.description:
This expression allows you to grab an item from NBT compounds.
This can be useful when wanting to grab items from file nbt, or nbt of an entity or an inventory holding block (like a chest or furnace).
It can also be useful for creating your own serializing system.
NOTE: Items previously serialized in MC versions 1.20.4 and below, will properly upgrade for MC 1.20.5.

en:
This expression allows you to grab an item from NBT compounds.
This can be useful when wanting to grab items from file nbt, or nbt of an entity or an inventory holding block (like a chest or furnace).
It can also be useful for creating your own serializing system.
NOTE: Items previously serialized in MC versions 1.20.4 and below, will properly upgrade for MC 1.20.5.

syntax_10690.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10690.title: NBT - Empty Compound
syntax_10690.description:
Returns an empty/new NBT compound.
Optionally you can define this as a section to modify the created nbt compound directly

en:
Returns an empty/new NBT compound.
Optionally you can define this as a section to modify the created nbt compound directly

syntax_10704.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10704.title: Player Health Scale
syntax_10704.description:
Represents the scaled health of the player sent to the client.
NOTE: This is not persistent, does not last restarts/relogs.
NOTE: displayedHealth = (health * 2) / (max health * 2) * scale.
NOTE: If the player is not currently health scaled, this will not be set.

en:
Represents the scaled health of the player sent to the client.
NOTE: This is not persistent, does not last restarts/relogs.
NOTE: displayedHealth = (health * 2) / (max health * 2) * scale.
NOTE: If the player is not currently health scaled, this will not be set.

syntax_10723.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10723.title: Recipe - Ingredients of Recipe
syntax_10723.description: Get the ingredients from a recipe.

en: Get the ingredients from a recipe.

syntax_10715.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10715.title: RayTrace - Hit Block
syntax_10715.description: Gets the hit block resulting from a RayTrace.

en: Gets the hit block resulting from a RayTrace.

syntax_10725.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10725.title: Recipe - Result
syntax_10725.description:
Get the result item of a recipe.
`ID` = Minecraft or custom NamespacedKey, see examples.

en:
Get the result item of a recipe.
`ID` = Minecraft or custom NamespacedKey, see examples.

syntax_10724.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10724.title: Recipe - Recipe Type
syntax_10724.description:
Get the type of a recipe.
`ID` = Minecraft or custom NamespacedKey, see examples.

en:
Get the type of a recipe.
`ID` = Minecraft or custom NamespacedKey, see examples.

syntax_10720.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10720.title: Recipe - All Recipes
syntax_10720.description:
Get a list of all recipes. May be from a specific item, may be just Minecraft recipes or custom recipes.
Due to some items having more than 1 recipe this may return multiple recipes.

en:
Get a list of all recipes. May be from a specific item, may be just Minecraft recipes or custom recipes.
Due to some items having more than 1 recipe this may return multiple recipes.

syntax_10721.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10721.title: Recipe - CookTime
syntax_10721.description: Get the cooktime of a cooking recipes.

en: Get the cooktime of a cooking recipes.

syntax_10750.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10750.title: Stored Enchantments
syntax_10750.description: Get/set the stored enchantments of an enchanted book.

en: Get/set the stored enchantments of an enchanted book.

syntax_10717.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10717.title: RayTrace - Hit Entity
syntax_10717.description: Gets the hit entity resulting from a RayTrace.

en: Gets the hit entity resulting from a RayTrace.

syntax_10726.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10726.title: Recipe Choice - Material Choice
syntax_10726.description:
A material choice is a list of items or a minecraft tag, that can be used as an option in some recipes.
Will return as a RecipeChoice.
When using the 'every' item type, this will grab all relatable items in a list, ie: 'every sword'.
This allows you to have one specific slot of a recipe to accept multiple items, without having to create multiple recipes.
Do note that material choices do not accept custom items (ie: items with names, lore, enchants, etc). Requires Minecraft 1.13+

en:
A material choice is a list of items or a minecraft tag, that can be used as an option in some recipes.
Will return as a RecipeChoice.
When using the 'every' item type, this will grab all relatable items in a list, ie: 'every sword'.
This allows you to have one specific slot of a recipe to accept multiple items, without having to create multiple recipes.
Do note that material choices do not accept custom items (ie: items with names, lore, enchants, etc). Requires Minecraft 1.13+

syntax_10722.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10722.title: Recipe - Experience
syntax_10722.description: Get the experience gained from a cooking recipe.

en: Get the experience gained from a cooking recipe.

syntax_10719.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10719.title: RayTrace - Hit Position
syntax_10719.description: Get the hit position resulting from a RayTrace (returns a vector).

en: Get the hit position resulting from a RayTrace (returns a vector).

syntax_10718.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10718.title: RayTrace - Hit Location
syntax_10718.description: Get the hit location resulting from a RayTrace.

en: Get the hit location resulting from a RayTrace.

syntax_10711.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10711.title: Quaternion - Rotated
syntax_10711.description: Rotate a quaternion along the XYZ axis using a vector.

en: Rotate a quaternion along the XYZ axis using a vector.

syntax_10712.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10712.title: Quaternion - Values
syntax_10712.description: Represents the x/y/z/w values of a quaternion.

en: Represents the x/y/z/w values of a quaternion.

syntax_10716.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10716.title: RayTrace - Hit BlockFace
syntax_10716.description: Gets the hit BlockFace resulting from a RayTrace.

en: Gets the hit BlockFace resulting from a RayTrace.

syntax_10710.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10710.title: Quaternion - From Vector
syntax_10710.description:
Create a quaternion from a vector.
The XYZ of the vector will represents the degrees the display entity will rotate on the XYZ axis.

en:
Create a quaternion from a vector.
The XYZ of the vector will represents the degrees the display entity will rotate on the XYZ axis.

syntax_10707.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10707.title: Potion Effect Duration
syntax_10707.description:
Get the duration of a potion effect. If running 1.19.4+ and the potion is infinite,
it will return as the max available time.

en:
Get the duration of a potion effect. If running 1.19.4+ and the potion is infinite,
it will return as the max available time.

syntax_10708.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10708.title: Potion Effect Tier
syntax_10708.description:
Get the tier of a potion effect.

Note: This is the Minecraft potion effect amplifier + 1, Skript adds 1 for some odd reason.

en:
Get the tier of a potion effect.

Note: This is the Minecraft potion effect amplifier + 1, Skript adds 1 for some odd reason.

syntax_10709.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10709.title: PufferFish - Puff State
syntax_10709.description: Represents the puff state of a puffer fish. An integer between 0 and 2

en: Represents the puff state of a puffer fish. An integer between 0 and 2

syntax_10733.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10733.title: Scoreboard - Objective Get
syntax_10733.description:
Get an already registered objective.
Optionally can input a scoreboard (will default to main scoreboard).

en:
Get an already registered objective.
Optionally can input a scoreboard (will default to main scoreboard).

syntax_10735.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10735.title: Scoreboard - Objective Score
syntax_10735.description:
Get/Set the score of an entity/string for an objective.
If the score has never been set, it'll return nothing.

en:
Get/Set the score of an entity/string for an objective.
If the score has never been set, it'll return nothing.

syntax_10746.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10746.title: Spawner - Spawn Delay
syntax_10746.description:
The delay between a spawner spawning a new entity.
Maximum default is 800 ticks (40 seconds).
Minimum default is 200 ticks (10 seconds).

en:
The delay between a spawner spawning a new entity.
Maximum default is 800 ticks (40 seconds).
Minimum default is 200 ticks (10 seconds).

syntax_10749.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10749.title: Statistic - Get/Set
syntax_10749.description: Represents the statistic of a Player. You can get, set, add, remove or reset stats.

en: Represents the statistic of a Player. You can get, set, add, remove or reset stats.

syntax_10738.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10738.title: Server Tick - Frozen State
syntax_10738.description:
Get/set the frozen tick state of the server.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

en:
Get/set the frozen tick state of the server.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10727.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10727.title: Regeneration Rate
syntax_10727.description:
Represents the regeneration rate (1 health per x ticks) of the Player.

Saturated = When they have saturation and their food level >= 20. Default is 10 ticks.

Unsaturated = When they have no saturation and their food level >= 18. Default is 80 ticks.

en:
Represents the regeneration rate (1 health per x ticks) of the Player.

Saturated = When they have saturation and their food level >= 20. Default is 10 ticks.

Unsaturated = When they have no saturation and their food level >= 18. Default is 80 ticks.

syntax_10728.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10728.title: Respawn Reason
syntax_10728.description: Represents the reason the respawn event was called. Requires MC 1.19.4+

en: Represents the reason the respawn event was called. Requires MC 1.19.4+

syntax_10740.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10740.title: Simulation Distance
syntax_10740.description:
Get/change simulation distance of a player/world.
This represents the distance (in chunks) that entities/blocks outside of this distance will not tick.

NOTE: Must be a value between 2 and 32.

en:
Get/change simulation distance of a player/world.
This represents the distance (in chunks) that entities/blocks outside of this distance will not tick.

NOTE: Must be a value between 2 and 32.

syntax_10737.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10737.title: Server Platform
syntax_10737.description:
Returns the platform the server is running on, ex: CraftBukkit, Spigot, Paper.

NOTE: This is returned from Skript itself, results may vary when not running a standard Bukkit based server.

en:
Returns the platform the server is running on, ex: CraftBukkit, Spigot, Paper.

NOTE: This is returned from Skript itself, results may vary when not running a standard Bukkit based server.

syntax_10748.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10748.title: Spellcaster Spell
syntax_10748.description: Get/set/reset the spell of a spell casting entity (Illusioner, Evoker).

en: Get/set/reset the spell of a spell casting entity (Illusioner, Evoker).

syntax_10743.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10743.title: Spawner - Max Nearby Entities
syntax_10743.description:
Set the new maximum amount of similar entities that are allowed to be within spawning range of a spawner.
If more than the maximum number of entities are within range, the spawner will not spawn and try again.
Default value is 16.
syntax_10743.syntax_pattern:
[the] max nearby [spawner] entities of %blocks%
%blocks%'[s] max nearby [spawner] entities

en:
Set the new maximum amount of similar entities that are allowed to be within spawning range of a spawner.
If more than the maximum number of entities are within range, the spawner will not spawn and try again.
Default value is 16.

syntax_10745.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10745.title: Spawner - Spawn Count
syntax_10745.description: Get how many mobs attempt to spawn. Default is 4.

en: Get how many mobs attempt to spawn. Default is 4.

syntax_10744.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10744.title: Spawner - Required Player Range
syntax_10744.description:
Get the maximum distance(squared) a player can be in order for a spawner to be active.
If this value is less than 0, the spawner is always active (given that there are players online).
Otherwise if the value is 0 the spawner is never active.
Default value is 16.

en:
Get the maximum distance(squared) a player can be in order for a spawner to be active.
If this value is less than 0, the spawner is always active (given that there are players online).
Otherwise if the value is 0 the spawner is never active.
Default value is 16.

syntax_10742.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10742.title: Spawn Egg from Entity
syntax_10742.description: Gets a spawn egg from an entity/entityType. Requires Paper, or Spigot 1.20.1+.

en: Gets a spawn egg from an entity/entityType. Requires Paper, or Spigot 1.20.1+.

syntax_10741.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10741.title: Skript Type of Object
syntax_10741.description:
Returns the Skript type (also known as 'ClassInfo') of an object.
Useful for debugging. Will return as a string.

en:
Returns the Skript type (also known as 'ClassInfo') of an object.
Useful for debugging. Will return as a string.

syntax_10760.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10760.title: Team - Color
syntax_10760.description: Get/set the color of a team.

en: Get/set the color of a team.

syntax_10764.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10764.title: Team - Options
syntax_10764.description: Represents an option for a team.

en: Represents an option for a team.

syntax_10756.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10756.title: Tab Completion Argument
syntax_10756.description: Get the argument or a list of all arguments in a tab complete event.

en: Get the argument or a list of all arguments in a tab complete event.

syntax_10755.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10755.title: Suspicious Block - Item
syntax_10755.description: Represents the item hiding in a Suspicious Block. Requires Minecraft 1.19.4+

en: Represents the item hiding in a Suspicious Block. Requires Minecraft 1.19.4+

syntax_10758.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10758.title: Target Block Exact
syntax_10758.description:
Returns the exact target block of a living entity.
Unlike Skript's target block expression, this takes the blocks' precise collision shapes into account.
An example would be a torch, Skript's expression won't account for the actual shape of the block.
Will also ignore fluids. Skript's expression is only for players, this one is for all living entities.

en:
Returns the exact target block of a living entity.
Unlike Skript's target block expression, this takes the blocks' precise collision shapes into account.
An example would be a torch, Skript's expression won't account for the actual shape of the block.
Will also ignore fluids. Skript's expression is only for players, this one is for all living entities.

syntax_10763.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10763.title: Team - Name
syntax_10763.description:
Represents the name and display name of a team.

NOTE: Display name can be set, name cannot be set.

en:
Represents the name and display name of a team.

NOTE: Display name can be set, name cannot be set.

syntax_10765.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10765.title: Team - Prefix/Suffix
syntax_10765.description: Get/set/delete the prefix/suffix of a team.

en: Get/set/delete the prefix/suffix of a team.

syntax_10766.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10766.title: Team - State
syntax_10766.description: Represents the friendly fire and can see friendly invisibles states of a team.
syntax_10766.syntax_pattern:
[the] (allow friendly fire|can see friendly invisibles) team state of %teams%
%teams%'[s] (allow friendly fire|can see friendly invisibles) team state

en: Represents the friendly fire and can see friendly invisibles states of a team.

syntax_10767.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10767.title: TextComponent - Async Chat Format/Message
syntax_10767.description:
Get/set the chat message from an async chat event as a text component.
Set the chat format of an async chat event (no getter for this).
Requires PaperMC!

en:
Get/set the chat message from an async chat event as a text component.
Set the chat format of an async chat event (no getter for this).
Requires PaperMC!

syntax_10768.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10768.title: TextComponent - Chest Inventory
syntax_10768.description: Create a chest inventory with a component name.
syntax_10768.syntax_pattern:
[a [new]] chest inventory with component name %textcomp% [with %-number% row[s]]
[a [new]] chest inventory with %number% row[s] with component name %textcomp%

en: Create a chest inventory with a component name.

syntax_10771.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10771.title: TextComponent - Click Event Of
syntax_10771.description: Set the click event of a text component.

en: Set the click event of a text component.

syntax_10757.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10757.title: Tab Completions
syntax_10757.description:
Set the tab completions used in a tab complete event.
You can specify which position in the command arguments also.
You can also remove texts from tab completions.

en:
Set the tab completions used in a tab complete event.
You can specify which position in the command arguments also.
You can also remove texts from tab completions.

syntax_10769.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10769.title: TextComponent - Children
syntax_10769.description: Get the children of a text component. This essentially splits the component up into its parts.

en: Get the children of a text component. This essentially splits the component up into its parts.

syntax_10785.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10785.title: Translation Key
syntax_10785.description: Gets the translation key from an object. Requires PaperMC.

en: Gets the translation key from an object. Requires PaperMC.

syntax_10777.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10777.title: TextComponent - Item Lore
syntax_10777.description: Get/set the lore of an item using text components.

en: Get/set the lore of an item using text components.

syntax_10784.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10784.title: TimeSpan - Numbers
syntax_10784.description: Get the ticks/seconds/minutes/hours of a timespan.

en: Get the ticks/seconds/minutes/hours of a timespan.
Get all registered machines.

syntax_10783.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10783.title: TextComponent - Team Prefix/Suffix
syntax_10783.description: Get/set prefix/suffix of teams using components.

en: Get/set prefix/suffix of teams using components.

syntax_10775.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10775.title: TextComponent - Hover Event Of
syntax_10775.description: Set the hover event of a text component.

en: Set the hover event of a text component.

syntax_10786.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10786.title: View Distance
syntax_10786.description:
Get/change view distance of a player/world.
This represents the distance (in chunks) that will be sent to the player.

NOTE: Must be a value between 2 and 32.

While I understand Skript has this, it's been disabled in Skript due to previous PaperMC issues.

en:
Get/change view distance of a player/world.
This represents the distance (in chunks) that will be sent to the player.

NOTE: Must be a value between 2 and 32.

While I understand Skript has this, it's been disabled in Skript due to previous PaperMC issues.

syntax_10791.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10791.title: VirtualFurnace - Create
syntax_10791.description: Create a virtual furnace. When properties are not defined, default vanilla Minecraft properties are used.

en: Create a virtual furnace. When properties are not defined, default vanilla Minecraft properties are used.

syntax_10772.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10772.title: TextComponent - Entity Name
syntax_10772.description:
Get/set the component name of an entity.
- `name` = The vanilla name of an Entity, or the player's profile name (cannot be changed).
- `custom name` = The custom name of an entity, will only show when the player's crosshair is pointed at them.
- `display name` = Same as custom name, but will always show.
syntax_10772.syntax_pattern:
[the] component entity [custom|display] name of %entities%
%entities%'[s] component entity [custom|display] name

en:
Get/set the component name of an entity.
- `name` = The vanilla name of an Entity, or the player's profile name (cannot be changed).
- `custom name` = The custom name of an entity, will only show when the player's crosshair is pointed at them.
- `display name` = Same as custom name, but will always show.

syntax_10782.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10782.title: TextComponent - Sign Line
syntax_10782.description:
Get/set lines of a sign with text components.
Optionally set the front/back of a sign. (Defaults to front)

en:
Get/set lines of a sign with text components.
Optionally set the front/back of a sign. (Defaults to front)

syntax_10779.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10779.title: TextComponent - Merge Components
syntax_10779.description: Merge multiple components into one. If adding strings/texts, they will be converted into components.

en: Merge multiple components into one. If adding strings/texts, they will be converted into components.

syntax_10795.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10795.title: VirtualFurnace - Inventory
syntax_10795.description: Get the inventory of a virtual furnace. Can be used to open for a player.

en: Get the inventory of a virtual furnace. Can be used to open for a player.

syntax_10797.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10797.title: VirtualFurnace - Machine from ID
syntax_10797.description: Get a virtual furnace from a uuid.

en: Get a virtual furnace from a uuid.

syntax_10798.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10798.title: VirtualFurnace - UUID
syntax_10798.description: Get the id/uuid of a virtual furnace.

en: Get the id/uuid of a virtual furnace.

syntax_10799.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10799.title: World AutoSave
syntax_10799.description:
Turn on/off world auto saving. This will prevent changes in the world to be saved to file.

This doesn't appear to work when the server stops, so you may need to manually unload your world.

en:
Turn on/off world auto saving. This will prevent changes in the world to be saved to file.

This doesn't appear to work when the server stops, so you may need to manually unload your world.

syntax_10796.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10796.title: VirtualFurnace - Machine Name
syntax_10796.description: Get/set the name of a virtual furnace.

en: Get/set the name of a virtual furnace.

syntax_10794.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10794.title: VirtualFurnace - Furnace Properties Create
syntax_10794.description: Create furnace properties to be used in a virtual furnace.
syntax_10794.syntax_pattern: [virtual] furnace properties with cook speed multiplier %number% and [with] fuel speed multiplier %number%

en: Create furnace properties to be used in a virtual furnace.

syntax_10792.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10792.title: VirtualFurnace - Default Furnace Properties
syntax_10792.description: Represents furnace properties that mimic vanilla Minecraft properties.

en: Represents furnace properties that mimic vanilla Minecraft properties.

syntax_10806.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10806.title: Advancement
syntax_10806.description: Represents an advancement. These CAN be parsed, see examples.

en: Represents an advancement. These CAN be parsed, see examples.

syntax_10807.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10807.title: ArmorTrim
syntax_10807.description:
Represents an armor trim that may be applied to an item.
Requires MC 1.19.4+

en:
Represents an armor trim that may be applied to an item.
Requires MC 1.19.4+

syntax_10815.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10815.title: Bound
syntax_10815.description: Represents a 3D bounding box between 2 points

en: Represents a 3D bounding box between 2 points

syntax_10810.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10810.title: BlockFace
syntax_10810.description:
Represents the face of a block.
NOTE: These are auto-generated and may differ between server versions, currently generated for Minecraft 26.1.2.
syntax_10810.type_usage: down_face, east_face, east_north_east_face, east_south_east_face, north_east_face, north_face, north_north_east_face, north_north_west_face, north_west_face, self_face, south_east_face, south_face, south_south_east_face, south_south_west_face, south_west_face, up_face, west_face, west_north_west_face, west_south_west_face

en:
Represents the face of a block.
NOTE: These are auto-generated and may differ between server versions, currently generated for Minecraft 26.1.2.

syntax_10818.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10818.title: Damage Source
syntax_10818.description: Represents a source of damage. Requires MC 1.20.4+

en: Represents a source of damage. Requires MC 1.20.4+

syntax_10813.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10813.title: BossBar Flag
syntax_10813.description: Represents the flag options of a BossBar.

en: Represents the flag options of a BossBar.

syntax_10814.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10814.title: BossBar Style
syntax_10814.description: Represents the style options of a BossBar.

en: Represents the style options of a BossBar.

syntax_10846.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10846.title: Scoreboard - Objective Display Slot
syntax_10846.description: Locations for displaying objectives to the player
syntax_10846.type_usage: below_name, player_list, sidebar, sidebar_team_aqua, sidebar_team_black, sidebar_team_blue, sidebar_team_dark_aqua, sidebar_team_dark_blue, sidebar_team_dark_gray, sidebar_team_dark_green, sidebar_team_dark_purple, sidebar_team_dark_red, sidebar_team_gold, sidebar_team_gray, sidebar_team_green, sidebar_team_light_purple, sidebar_team_red, sidebar_team_white, sidebar_team_yellow

en: Locations for displaying objectives to the player

syntax_10817.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10817.title: Chunk Load Level
syntax_10817.description:
Represents the types of load levels of a chunk.
- `border_level` = Most game logic is not processed, including entities and redstone.
- `entity_ticking_level` = All game logic is processed.
- `inaccessible_level` = No game logic is processed, world generation may still occur.
- `ticking_level` = All game logic except entities is processed.
- `unloaded_level` = This chunk is not loaded.

en:
Represents the types of load levels of a chunk.
- `border_level` = Most game logic is not processed, including entities and redstone.
- `entity_ticking_level` = All game logic is processed.
- `inaccessible_level` = No game logic is processed, world generation may still occur.
- `ticking_level` = All game logic except entities is processed.
- `unloaded_level` = This chunk is not loaded.

syntax_10826.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10826.title: Fish Event State
syntax_10826.type_usage: bite, caught_entity, caught_fish, failed_attempt, fishing, in_ground, lured, reel_in

syntax_10816.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10816.title: Bukkit Color
syntax_10816.description:
Represents a Bukkit color. This is different than a Skript color
as it adds an alpha channel.

en:
Represents a Bukkit color. This is different than a Skript color
as it adds an alpha channel.

syntax_10812.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10812.title: BossBar
syntax_10812.description:
Represents a BossBar. Either from an entity or a custom one.
Players can be added to/removed from BossBars.
Custom BossBars can be deleted, BossBars of entities cannot be deleted.
NOTE: BossBars from entities cannot be saved in global variables, as the entity may not be loaded
on the server when that variable is trying to load. Custom BossBars can be saved in variables.

en:
Represents a BossBar. Either from an entity or a custom one.
Players can be added to/removed from BossBars.
Custom BossBars can be deleted, BossBars of entities cannot be deleted.
NOTE: BossBars from entities cannot be saved in global variables, as the entity may not be loaded
on the server when that variable is trying to load. Custom BossBars can be saved in variables.

syntax_10834.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10834.title: NBT - Compound
syntax_10834.description:
Represents the NBT compound of an entity/block/item/file/string.
NBT compounds can be merged by adding together, see examples.

en:
Represents the NBT compound of an entity/block/item/file/string.
NBT compounds can be merged by adding together, see examples.

syntax_10840.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10840.title: RayTrace - Result
syntax_10840.description:
The hit result of a ray trace.
Only the hit position is guaranteed to always be available.
The availability of the other attributes depends on what got hit
and on the context in which the ray trace was performed.

en:
The hit result of a ray trace.
Only the hit position is guaranteed to always be available.
The availability of the other attributes depends on what got hit
and on the context in which the ray trace was performed.
Represents the types of recipes.

syntax_10845.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10845.title: Scoreboard - Objective
syntax_10845.description:
Represents an objective in a scoreboard.
When deleting, the objective will be unregistered.
See [**Objectives**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Scoreboard#Objectives) on McWiki for more info.

en:
Represents an objective in a scoreboard.
When deleting, the objective will be unregistered.
See [**Objectives**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Scoreboard#Objectives) on McWiki for more info.

syntax_10844.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10844.title: Scoreboard - Criteria
syntax_10844.description:
Represents a criteria for a scoreboard objective.
See [**Criteria**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Scoreboard#Criteria) on McWiki for more info.

syntax_10879.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10879.title: Recipe - Register Shapeless Recipe
syntax_10879.description:
This section allows you to register a shapeless recipe and add ingredients.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting item of this recipe.
 - `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) (optional).
 - `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in (optional) Options are "building", "redstone", "equiptment", "misc".
 - `ingredients` = This section is where you will add the ingredients.
syntax_10879.entries: [{"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "result", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "group", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "category", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "ingredients", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}]

en:
This section allows you to register a shapeless recipe and add ingredients.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting item of this recipe.
 - `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) (optional).
 - `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in (optional) Options are "building", "redstone", "equiptment", "misc".
 - `ingredients` = This section is where you will add the ingredients.

syntax_10880.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10880.title: Recipe - Register Smithing Recipe
syntax_10880.description:
This section allows you to register a smithing transform recipe, define the output as well as the template, 
base and addition items
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe.
 - `result` = The resulting ItemStack of this recipe.
 - `template` = Represents the first slot in the smithing inventory (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice).
 - `base` = Represents the second slot in the smithing inventory (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice).
 - `addition` = Represents the third slot in the smithing inventory (Optional).
 - `copynbt` = Represents whether to copy the nbt from the input base item to the output, default = true (Requires PaperMC) (Optional).
syntax_10880.entries: [{"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "result", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "template", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "base", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "addition", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "copynbt", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
This section allows you to register a smithing transform recipe, define the output as well as the template, 
base and addition items
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe.
 - `result` = The resulting ItemStack of this recipe.
 - `template` = Represents the first slot in the smithing inventory (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice).
 - `base` = Represents the second slot in the smithing inventory (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice).
 - `addition` = Represents the third slot in the smithing inventory (Optional).
 - `copynbt` = Represents whether to copy the nbt from the input base item to the output, default = true (Requires PaperMC) (Optional).

syntax_11060.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11060.title: Entity Inside Block
syntax_11060.description:
Called when an entity enters the hitbox of a block.
Only called for blocks that react when an entity is inside.
If cancelled, any action that would have resulted from that entity being in the block will not happen (such as extinguishing an entity in a cauldron).
Currently called for: Big dripleaf, Bubble column, Buttons, Cactus, Campfire, Cauldron, Crops, Ender Portal, Fires, Frogspawn, Honey, Hopper, Detector rails,
Nether portals, Pitcher crop, Powdered snow, Pressure plates, Sweet berry bush, Tripwire, Waterlily, Web, Wither rose
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-block`
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`

The two numbers represent a chunk's X/Y coords, NOT a location. A chunk's X/Y coords are basically
a location divided by 16. Ex: Chunk 1/1 would be at location X=16, Z=16.

The two numbers represent a chunk's X/Y coords, NOT a location. A chunk's X/Y coords are basically

syntax_11063.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11063.title: LootTable - Fill Inventory
syntax_11063.description:
Fill an inventory with a predefined LootTable.
This inventory must belong to a block or entity (custom inventories will not work).
Optionals:
Some loot tables will require some of these values whereas others may not.
`seed` = Represents the random seed used to generate loot (if not provided will generate randomly).
`looting modifier` = Set the looting enchant level equivalent to use when generating loot.
Values less than or equal to 0 will force the LootTable to only return a single Item per pool.
`luck` = How much luck to have when generating loot.
`killer/looter` = The Player that killed/looted. This Player will be used to get the looting level if looting modifier is not set.
`looted entity` = The Entity that was killed/looted.
syntax_11063.syntax_pattern: fill %inventories% from %loottable% [with seed %number%] [with looting modifier %number%] [with luck %number%] [with (killer|looter) %player%] [with looted entity %entity%]

en:
Fill an inventory with a predefined LootTable.
This inventory must belong to a block or entity (custom inventories will not work).
Optionals:
Some loot tables will require some of these values whereas others may not.
`seed` = Represents the random seed used to generate loot (if not provided will generate randomly).
`looting modifier` = Set the looting enchant level equivalent to use when generating loot.
Values less than or equal to 0 will force the LootTable to only return a single Item per pool.
`luck` = How much luck to have when generating loot.
`killer/looter` = The Player that killed/looted. This Player will be used to get the looting level if looting modifier is not set.
`looted entity` = The Entity that was killed/looted.

syntax_10881.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10881.title: Task - Run Task Later
syntax_10881.description:
Run a task later. Similar to Skript's delay effect, with the difference being everything in the
section is run later. All code after your section will keep running as normal without a delay.
This can be very useful in loops, to prevent halting the loop.
You can optionally have your task repeat until cancelled.
You can optionally run your code async/on another thread.
You can optionally store the task ID in a variable, to help make use of it later.

**NOTE**: A good chunk of Bukkit/Minecraft stuff can NOT be run async. It may throw console errors.
Please be careful when running async, this is generally reserved for heavy math/functions that could cause lag.
Simply waiting a tick, or running a new non-async section will put your code back on the main thread.

**Patterns**:
The 2nd pattern is only of concern if you are running Folia or have Paper schedulers enabled in the config, otherwise just use the first pattern.
- `globally` = Will run this task on the global scheduler.
- `for %entity` = Will run this task for an entity, will follow the entity around (region wise)and will cancel itself when the entity is no longer valid.
- `at %location%` = Will run this task at a specific location (Use this for block changes in this section).
syntax_10881.syntax_pattern:
[async] (run|execute) [task] %timespan% later [repeating every %-timespan%] [globally] [and store [task] id in %-object%]
[async] (run|execute) [task] %timespan% later [repeating every %-timespan%] [(at|on|for) %-entity/location%] [and store [task] id in %-object%]

en:
Run a task later. Similar to Skript's delay effect, with the difference being everything in the
section is run later. All code after your section will keep running as normal without a delay.
This can be very useful in loops, to prevent halting the loop.
You can optionally have your task repeat until cancelled.
You can optionally run your code async/on another thread.
You can optionally store the task ID in a variable, to help make use of it later.

**NOTE**: A good chunk of Bukkit/Minecraft stuff can NOT be run async. It may throw console errors.
Please be careful when running async, this is generally reserved for heavy math/functions that could cause lag.
Simply waiting a tick, or running a new non-async section will put your code back on the main thread.

**Patterns**:
The 2nd pattern is only of concern if you are running Folia or have Paper schedulers enabled in the config, otherwise just use the first pattern.
- `globally` = Will run this task on the global scheduler.
- `for %entity` = Will run this task for an entity, will follow the entity around (region wise)and will cancel itself when the entity is no longer valid.
- `at %location%` = Will run this task at a specific location (Use this for block changes in this section).

syntax_10877.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10877.title: Recipe - Register Cooking Recipe
syntax_10877.description:
This section allows you to register any cooking recipe and define special properties.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting ItemStack of this recipe.
 - `input` = The item the recipe requires as an input to output the result (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice) (Required).
 - `cooktime` = How long the recipe will take to finish cooking before result is given (Optional).
 - `experience` = The amount of experience gained when the recipe is finished cooking (Optional) Default cook times are, furnace = 10 seconds, smoking/blasting = 5 seconds and campfire = 30 seconds.
 - `group` = You can define a group in which all recipes under this are sorted together in the recipe book (Optional). Examples of this in game are beds and wood types.
 - `category` = Which category in the recipe book this recipe should appear within (Optional 1.19.4+). Valid category types are "food", "blocks", "misc", if no category is defined it defaults to "misc".
syntax_10877.entries: [{"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "result", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "input", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "group", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "cooktime", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "experience", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "category", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
This section allows you to register any cooking recipe and define special properties.
**Entries**:
 - `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
 - `result` = The resulting ItemStack of this recipe.
 - `input` = The item the recipe requires as an input to output the result (Accepts an ItemStack or RecipeChoice) (Required).
 - `cooktime` = How long the recipe will take to finish cooking before result is given (Optional).
 - `experience` = The amount of experience gained when the recipe is finished cooking (Optional) Default cook times are, furnace = 10 seconds, smoking/blasting = 5 seconds and campfire = 30 seconds.
 - `group` = You can define a group in which all recipes under this are sorted together in the recipe book (Optional). Examples of this in game are beds and wood types.
 - `category` = Which category in the recipe book this recipe should appear within (Optional 1.19.4+). Valid category types are "food", "blocks", "misc", if no category is defined it defaults to "misc".

syntax_10530.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10530.title: Particle Spawn
syntax_10530.description:
Spawn a [**particle**](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=14494).
This system is more inline with how Bukkit deals with particles, hence the choices in the pattern.
Some particles may be affected differently by these values, so let's break them down:
`first number` = count, how many particles to spawn at once. (use '0' if you notice the particle kinda flies away.)
`particle` = the particle to spawn.
`using` = the data used for this particle (some particles like 'block', 'item' and 'dust' require more data).
`location` = where you are going to spawn the particle.
`delta` = a vector with the maximum random offset. The position of each particle will be randomized positively and negatively by the offset parameters on each axis.
Some particles use the delta to set color/direction if count is set to 0.
`extra` = the extra data for this particle, depends on the particle used (normally speed).
`force` = whether to send the particle to players within an extended range and encourage 
their client to render it regardless of settings (this only works when not using `for player[s]`) (default = false)
`for %players%` = will only send this particle to a player, not the whole server.

en:
Spawn a [**particle**](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=14494).
This system is more inline with how Bukkit deals with particles, hence the choices in the pattern.
Some particles may be affected differently by these values, so let's break them down:
`first number` = count, how many particles to spawn at once. (use '0' if you notice the particle kinda flies away.)
`particle` = the particle to spawn.
`using` = the data used for this particle (some particles like 'block', 'item' and 'dust' require more data).
`location` = where you are going to spawn the particle.
`delta` = a vector with the maximum random offset. The position of each particle will be randomized positively and negatively by the offset parameters on each axis.
Some particles use the delta to set color/direction if count is set to 0.
`extra` = the extra data for this particle, depends on the particle used (normally speed).
`force` = whether to send the particle to players within an extended range and encourage 
their client to render it regardless of settings (this only works when not using `for player[s]`) (default = false)
`for %players%` = will only send this particle to a player, not the whole server.

syntax_10556.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10556.title: TextComponent - Send
syntax_10556.description:
Send text components to audiences. You can also broadcast components as well.
As of 1.16.0 you can also send action bar components to players and you can also send normal strings.
`to %audiences%` = An audience is anything that can receieve a component (players, entities, console, worlds, server, etc).

The optional sender (supported in Minecraft 1.16.4+) allows you to send components from a specific player.
This is useful to make sure players can block messages using MC 1.16.4's new player chat ignore system.

en:
Send text components to audiences. You can also broadcast components as well.
As of 1.16.0 you can also send action bar components to players and you can also send normal strings.
`to %audiences%` = An audience is anything that can receieve a component (players, entities, console, worlds, server, etc).

The optional sender (supported in Minecraft 1.16.4+) allows you to send components from a specific player.
This is useful to make sure players can block messages using MC 1.16.4's new player chat ignore system.

syntax_10498.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10498.title: Server Tick - Is Entity Frozen
syntax_10498.description:
Checks if a given entity is frozen.
Entities will only be frozen if 3 criteria are met:

 - Server is frozen (via `/tick freeze` command or frozen state expression).

 - Entity is not a player.

 - Entity has no player passengers.

I understand the syntax is a bit silly but it's to prevent collission with Skript's `%entity% is frozen` condition.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.
syntax_10498.syntax_pattern:
%entities% (is|are) tick frozen
%entities% (isn't|is not|aren't|are not) tick frozen

en:
Checks if a given entity is frozen.
Entities will only be frozen if 3 criteria are met:

 - Server is frozen (via `/tick freeze` command or frozen state expression).

 - Entity is not a player.

 - Entity has no player passengers.

I understand the syntax is a bit silly but it's to prevent collission with Skript's `%entity% is frozen` condition.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_10739.title: Server Tick - Tick Rate
syntax_10739.description:
Represents the tick rate of the server. Can be a value from `1.0` to `10000.0`.
Supports fractional numbers, see examples.
Default = 20.
See [**Tick Command**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Commands/tick) on McWiki for more details.

en:

syntax_10608.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10608.title: Bound - Values
syntax_10608.description:
Get/set/delete custom values for bounds.
Some objects will be serialized as their type, others will serialize as just a string.
Numbers/Locations/Booleans/(Offline)Players/Items will be serialized as their type.
Entities will be serialized as a string version of their UUID.
All other objects will be serialized as a string.
All bound values will return a list of the values, all bound keys will return a list of keys for these values.
Deleting all bound values/keys will clear the list.

en:
Get/set/delete custom values for bounds.
Some objects will be serialized as their type, others will serialize as just a string.
Numbers/Locations/Booleans/(Offline)Players/Items will be serialized as their type.
Entities will be serialized as a string version of their UUID.
All other objects will be serialized as a string.
All bound values will return a list of the values, all bound keys will return a list of keys for these values.
Deleting all bound values/keys will clear the list.

syntax_10522.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10522.title: Load/Unload/Delete World
syntax_10522.description:
Load a world from a world creator.
Worlds created/loaded with a world creator, are saved in the 'plugins/SkBee/worlds.yml' file
and automatically loaded on server start if auto-load is enabled in the config.
If you wish to import a world, just use a world creator with a name that matches that world folder.
You can load a world from a name (assuming this world is in your world directory and not loaded).
You can unload a world with an option to save/not save (defaults to saving).
You can also delete a world, but only a custom world.
syntax_10522.syntax_pattern:
load world from [[world] creator] %worldcreator%
load world %namespacedkey%
unload [world] %world% [and (save|(do not|don't) save)]
delete world file for [world] %string%
delete world file for world with key %namespacedkey%

en:
Load a world from a world creator.
Worlds created/loaded with a world creator, are saved in the 'plugins/SkBee/worlds.yml' file
and automatically loaded on server start if auto-load is enabled in the config.
If you wish to import a world, just use a world creator with a name that matches that world folder.
You can load a world from a name (assuming this world is in your world directory and not loaded).
You can unload a world with an option to save/not save (defaults to saving).
You can also delete a world, but only a custom world.

syntax_10781.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10781.title: TextComponent - New Text Component
syntax_10781.description:
Create a new text component. Can add hover/click events to it.

**Types:**
Text: Just a plain old text component from a string.
Rawtext: Same as text, but color codes will be visible.
Keybind: Will use Minecraft's keybind system.
Translate: Will use Minecraft's lang file keys.
  - You can find these in your Minecraft jar `assets/minecraft/lang/<lang file>.json`.
  - Also supports getting translations for objects such as ItemTypes, Entities and PotionEffectTypes.
  - When sent to the client, the client will translate based on the lang they've picked.
  - Some lang file entries take in other arguments, that's what the optional `using args %objects%` is for.
  - Optionally you can add a fallback, this is the text sent to the client if the client cannot find the translation key.

Json: Will deserialize a json string back into a component.
  - Minecraft stores components in NBT as json components (ex: name of a held item).
syntax_10781.syntax_pattern:
[a] [new] (text|rawtext) component[s] (from|of) %strings%
[a] [new] key[ ]bind component[s] (from|of) %strings%
[a] [new] translate component[s] (from|of) %objects% [(with|using) arg[ument]s %-objects%] [with fallback %-string%]
[a] [new] json component (from|of) %strings%
[a] [new] empty [text] component

en:
Create a new text component. Can add hover/click events to it.

**Types:**
Text: Just a plain old text component from a string.
Rawtext: Same as text, but color codes will be visible.
Keybind: Will use Minecraft's keybind system.
Translate: Will use Minecraft's lang file keys.
  - You can find these in your Minecraft jar `assets/minecraft/lang/<lang file>.json`.
  - Also supports getting translations for objects such as ItemTypes, Entities and PotionEffectTypes.
  - When sent to the client, the client will translate based on the lang they've picked.
  - Some lang file entries take in other arguments, that's what the optional `using args %objects%` is for.
  - Optionally you can add a fallback, this is the text sent to the client if the client cannot find the translation key.

Json: Will deserialize a json string back into a component.
  - Minecraft stores components in NBT as json components (ex: name of a held item).

syntax_14862.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14862.title: Entity Place
syntax_14862.description:
Triggered when an entity is created in the world by a player "placing" an item on a block.
Note that this event is currently only fired for four specific placements: armor stands, boats, minecarts, and end crystals.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-block`
   - **Description**: Gets the block the entity was placed on.
 - `event-blockface`
   - **Description**: Gets the face of the block the entity was placed on.
 - `event-equipment slot`
   - **Description**: Gets the hand used to place the entity.
 - `event-player`
   - **Description**: Gets the player who placed the entity.
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
syntax_14862.event_values: event-block, event-blockface, event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity type, event-equipment slot, event-location, event-player, event-world

en:
Triggered when an entity is created in the world by a player "placing" an item on a block.
Note that this event is currently only fired for four specific placements: armor stands, boats, minecarts, and end crystals.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-block`
   - **Description**: Gets the block the entity was placed on.
 - `event-blockface`
   - **Description**: Gets the face of the block the entity was placed on.
 - `event-equipment slot`
   - **Description**: Gets the hand used to place the entity.
 - `event-player`
   - **Description**: Gets the player who placed the entity.
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`

syntax_12878.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12878.title: TextComponent - Tooltip Lines
syntax_12878.description:
Computes the tooltip lines of an item and returns as a list of text components.
**Disclaimer**: Tooltip contents are not guaranteed to be consistent across different Minecraft versions.
`advanced` = Whether the context is for advanced tooltips.
`creative` = Whether the context is for the creative inventory.
`for player` = Creates player specific tooltips.

en:
Computes the tooltip lines of an item and returns as a list of text components.
**Disclaimer**: Tooltip contents are not guaranteed to be consistent across different Minecraft versions.
`advanced` = Whether the context is for advanced tooltips.
`creative` = Whether the context is for the creative inventory.
`for player` = Creates player specific tooltips.

syntax_10539.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10539.title: Recipe - Smithing
syntax_10539.description:
Register a new smithing recipe.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
Note: While 'custom' items will work in these recipes, it appears the smithing table will not recognize them. Requires MC 1.16+

NOTE:Temporarily removed in 1.20+ as Minecraft has changed how these recipes work!
syntax_10539.syntax_pattern: register [new] smithing recipe for %itemtype% using %itemtype/recipechoice% and %itemtype/recipechoice% with id %string%

en:
Register a new smithing recipe.
The ID will be the name given to this recipe. IDs may only contain letters, numbers, periods, hyphens, a single colon and underscores,
NOT SPACES!!! By default, if no namespace is provided, recipes will start with the namespace "minecraft:",
this can be changed in the config to whatever you want. IDs are used for recipe discovery/unlocking recipes for players.
Note: While 'custom' items will work in these recipes, it appears the smithing table will not recognize them. Requires MC 1.16+

NOTE:Temporarily removed in 1.20+ as Minecraft has changed how these recipes work!

syntax_12866.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12866.title: ItemComponent - Glider
syntax_12866.description:
If applied, allows players to glide (as with elytra) when equipped.
If the item does not have a glider, it will return null, not false.
See [**Glider Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#glider) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = If set to true, a glider will be applied, otherwise removed.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

en:
If applied, allows players to glide (as with elytra) when equipped.
If the item does not have a glider, it will return null, not false.
See [**Glider Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#glider) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = If set to true, a glider will be applied, otherwise removed.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

syntax_10510.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10510.title: Bound - Resize
syntax_10510.description:
Resize a current bound.
Full will mark the bound to use the lowest/highest points of the world.
The second pattern will mark as a full bound without changing the locations.

NOTE: World of a bound cannot be changed.

**SPECIAL NOTE**:
- When using locations = The bound resizing will use the locations you pass thru
- When using blocks = The bound resizing will extend the x/y/z axes by 1 to fully include those blocks.
syntax_10510.syntax_pattern:
resize [full] bound %bound% (within|between) %block/location% and %block/location%
resize full bound %bound%

en:
Resize a current bound.
Full will mark the bound to use the lowest/highest points of the world.
The second pattern will mark as a full bound without changing the locations.

NOTE: World of a bound cannot be changed.

**SPECIAL NOTE**:
- When using locations = The bound resizing will use the locations you pass thru
- When using blocks = The bound resizing will extend the x/y/z axes by 1 to fully include those blocks.

syntax_12865.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12865.title: ItemComponent - Enchantable
syntax_12865.description:
If present, and applicable enchantments are available, items with the component can be enchanted in an enchanting table.
Positive integer representing the item's enchantability. A higher value allows enchantments with a higher cost to be picked.
See [**Enchantable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#enchantable) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = Allows you to set the enchantability.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

syntax_10780.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10780.title: TextComponent - MiniMessage
syntax_10780.description:
Get a mini message from a string.
These messages are still components, which you can still apply hover/click events to.
You can also add optional tag resolvers. Essential you create a resolver to replace `<someString>` 
in mini message with something else (See examples for more details).
For more info check out the [**Mini Message Format**](https://docs.papermc.io/adventure/minimessage/format/) page.

These messages are still components, which you can still apply hover/click events to.
You can also add optional tag resolvers. Essential you create a resolver to replace `<someString>` 
in mini message with something else (See examples for more details).
For more info check out the [**Mini Message Format**](https://docs.papermc.io/adventure/minimessage/format/) page.

syntax_12872.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12872.title: Recipe - Crafting Result From Items
syntax_12872.description:
Get the result of a crafting recipe from a list of items.
This requires either 4 or 9 items, to follow the 2x2 and 3x3 crafting grids respectively.
Use `air` to represent blank slots in the crafting grid.
I have no clue what the world is for since recipes are not per world, but Bukkit offers it so here we are.

en:
Get the result of a crafting recipe from a list of items.
This requires either 4 or 9 items, to follow the 2x2 and 3x3 crafting grids respectively.
Use `air` to represent blank slots in the crafting grid.
I have no clue what the world is for since recipes are not per world, but Bukkit offers it so here we are.

syntax_12897.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12897.title: ItemComponent - Firework Explosion Component Apply
syntax_12897.description:
Apply a firework explosion effect to a firework star.
This can also be used within the Fireworks Component's `explosions:` section.
See [**Firework Explosion Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#firework_explosion) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `shape` = The [firework type](https://docs.skriptlang.org/classes.html#FireworkType) of the explosion.
- `colors` = The colors of the initial particles of the explosion, randomly selected from.
- `fade_colors` = The colors of the fading particles of the explosion, randomly selected from.
- `has_trail` = Whether or not the explosion has a trail effect (diamond).
- `has_twinkle` = Whether or not the explosion has a twinkle effect (glowstone dust).
syntax_12897.entries: [{"name": "shape", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "colors", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "fade_colors", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "has_trail", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "has_twinkle", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply a firework explosion effect to a firework star.
This can also be used within the Fireworks Component's `explosions:` section.
See [**Firework Explosion Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#firework_explosion) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `shape` = The [firework type](https://docs.skriptlang.org/classes.html#FireworkType) of the explosion.
- `colors` = The colors of the initial particles of the explosion, randomly selected from.
- `fade_colors` = The colors of the fading particles of the explosion, randomly selected from.
- `has_trail` = Whether or not the explosion has a trail effect (diamond).
- `has_twinkle` = Whether or not the explosion has a twinkle effect (glowstone dust).

syntax_12898.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12898.title: ItemComponent - Fireworks Component Apply

Apply a fireworks component to a firework rocket.

**Entries**:

- `explosions` = A section to apply firework explosions (see 'ItemComponent - Firework Explosion Component Apply' section).

en:

syntax_12899.title: ItemComponent - Instrument Component Apply

syntax_12901.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12901.title: ItemComponent - Use Cooldown
syntax_12901.description:
Apply a cooldown to all items of the same type when it has been used.
See [**Use Cooldown Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#use_cooldown) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries**:
- `seconds` = The cooldown duration (timespan, must be >= 1 tick).
- `group` = The unique key to identify this cooldown group. If present, the item is included in a cooldown group and no longer shares cooldowns with its base item type, but instead with any other items that are part of the same cooldown group. [Optional]

en:
Apply a cooldown to all items of the same type when it has been used.
See [**Use Cooldown Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#use_cooldown) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries**:
- `seconds` = The cooldown duration (timespan, must be >= 1 tick).
- `group` = The unique key to identify this cooldown group. If present, the item is included in a cooldown group and no longer shares cooldowns with its base item type, but instead with any other items that are part of the same cooldown group. [Optional]

syntax_12902.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12902.title: Recipe - Register Transmute Recipe
syntax_12902.description:
Represents a recipe which will change the type of the input material when combined with an additional material, but preserve all custom data. 
Only the material of the result stack will be used.
Used for dyeing shulker boxes in Vanilla.
See [**crafting_transmute**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Recipe#crafting_transmute) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
- `result` = The material that will be transmuted in the result slot.
- `input` = The input ingredient (The item which will have it's data copied to the result).
- `material` = The item to be applied to the first (like a dye).
- `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) [optional].
- `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in [optional]. Options are "building", "redstone", "equipment", "misc".
syntax_12902.entries: [{"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "result", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "input", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "material", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "group", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "category", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Represents a recipe which will change the type of the input material when combined with an additional material, but preserve all custom data. 
Only the material of the result stack will be used.
Used for dyeing shulker boxes in Vanilla.
See [**crafting_transmute**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Recipe#crafting_transmute) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `id` = The ID for your recipe. This is used for recipe discovery and Minecraft's /recipe command.
- `result` = The material that will be transmuted in the result slot.
- `input` = The input ingredient (The item which will have it's data copied to the result).
- `material` = The item to be applied to the first (like a dye).
- `group` = Define a group to group your recipes together in the recipe book (an example would be having 3 recipes with the same outcome but a variety of ingredients) [optional].
- `category` = The recipe book category your recipe will be in [optional]. Options are "building", "redstone", "equipment", "misc".

syntax_12904.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12904.title: SwitchCase - Switch
syntax_12904.description: Switch an object and use cases to manage different actions.

syntax_11823.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11823.title: ItemComponent - Tool Rule Apply
syntax_11823.description:
Apply rules to a tool component. You can add as many as you'd like.
See [**McWiki Tool Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tool) for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
NOTE: One of either `block_types` or `block_tag` MUST be used.
`blocks` = The blocks to match for this rule to apply (Supports ItemTypes, BlockDatas, Minecraft Tags and TagKeys).
`speed` = If the blocks match, overrides the default mining speed (Must be a positive number). [Optional]
`correct for drops` = If the blocks match, overrides whether or not this tool is considered correct to mine at its most efficient speed, and to drop items if the block's loot table requires it. [Optional]
syntax_11823.entries: [{"name": "blocks", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "speed", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "correct_for_drops", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply rules to a tool component. You can add as many as you'd like.
See [**McWiki Tool Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tool) for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
NOTE: One of either `block_types` or `block_tag` MUST be used.
`blocks` = The blocks to match for this rule to apply (Supports ItemTypes, BlockDatas, Minecraft Tags and TagKeys).
`speed` = If the blocks match, overrides the default mining speed (Must be a positive number). [Optional]
`correct for drops` = If the blocks match, overrides whether or not this tool is considered correct to mine at its most efficient speed, and to drop items if the block's loot table requires it. [Optional]

syntax_12860.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12860.title: SwitchCase - Case Inline
syntax_12860.description:
Inline version of case section where you can return/run an effect all in one line.
Multiple objects are supported in cases.
- **Switch Expression** = Return an object based on the case matching the switched value.
- **Switch Section** = Run 1 effect based on the case matching the switched value.
Default will run if all other cases fail to match. Default must go last or all cases after it will be ignored.

en:
Inline version of case section where you can return/run an effect all in one line.
Multiple objects are supported in cases.
- **Switch Expression** = Return an object based on the case matching the switched value.
- **Switch Section** = Run 1 effect based on the case matching the switched value.
Default will run if all other cases fail to match. Default must go last or all cases after it will be ignored.

syntax_12903.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12903.title: SwitchCase - Case
syntax_12903.description:
In a switch section/expression, you can manage different actions based on the case matching the switched value.
Multiple objects are supported in cases.
Default will run if all other cases fail to match. Default must go last or all cases after it will be ignored.

en:
In a switch section/expression, you can manage different actions based on the case matching the switched value.
