Default will run if all other cases fail to match. Default must go last or all cases after it will be ignored.

syntax_12911.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12911.title: ItemComponent - Apply Effects
syntax_12911.description:
Used to apply potion/consume effects in a potion contents's `custom_effects` section, death protection's' `death_effects` section and consumable's `on_consume_effects` section.

**Patterns**:
- `%potioneffects%` = Used to apply a potion effect in a potion contents section.
- `%potioneffects% with probability %-number%` = Used to apply a potion/consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `remove effects %potioneffecttypes/typedkeys%` = Used to apply a `remove effects` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `clear all effects` = Used to apply a `clear all effects` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `teleport randomly within [[a] diameter [of]] %number% [blocks|meters]` = Used to apply a `teleport randomly` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `play sound %string/typedkey%` - Used to apply a `play sound` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
syntax_12911.syntax_pattern:
apply -> %potioneffects%
apply -> %potioneffects% with probability %-number%
apply -> remove effects %potioneffecttypes/typedkeys%
apply -> clear all effects
apply -> teleport randomly within [[a] diameter [of]] %number% [blocks|meters]
apply -> play sound %string/typedkey%

en:
Used to apply potion/consume effects in a potion contents's `custom_effects` section, death protection's' `death_effects` section and consumable's `on_consume_effects` section.

**Patterns**:
- `%potioneffects%` = Used to apply a potion effect in a potion contents section.
- `%potioneffects% with probability %-number%` = Used to apply a potion/consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `remove effects %potioneffecttypes/typedkeys%` = Used to apply a `remove effects` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `clear all effects` = Used to apply a `clear all effects` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `teleport randomly within [[a] diameter [of]] %number% [blocks|meters]` = Used to apply a `teleport randomly` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.
- `play sound %string/typedkey%` - Used to apply a `play sound` consume effect in a death protection/consumable section.

syntax_12867.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12867.title: ItemComponent - Intangible Projectile
syntax_12867.description:
If applied, a projectile item can't be picked up by a player when fired, except in creative mode.
If the item does not have this component, it will return null, not false.
See [**Intangible Projectile Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#intangible_projectile) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = If set to true, the component will be applied, otherwise removed.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).
syntax_12867.syntax_pattern:
[the] intangible projectile component of %itemstacks/itemtypes/slots%
%itemstacks/itemtypes/slots%'[s] intangible projectile component

en:
If applied, a projectile item can't be picked up by a player when fired, except in creative mode.
If the item does not have this component, it will return null, not false.
See [**Intangible Projectile Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#intangible_projectile) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = If set to true, the component will be applied, otherwise removed.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

syntax_12895.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12895.title: ItemComponent - Death Protection Component Apply
syntax_12895.description:
Apply a death protection component to an item.
If present, this item protects the holder from dying by restoring a single health point.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Death Protection Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#death_protection) on McWiki for more info.
`death_effect` = A `consume effect` to by applied to the component (supports a list) [Optional].
`death_effects` = A section to apply `consume effects` [Optional].

en:
Apply a death protection component to an item.
If present, this item protects the holder from dying by restoring a single health point.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Death Protection Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#death_protection) on McWiki for more info.
`death_effect` = A `consume effect` to by applied to the component (supports a list) [Optional].
`death_effects` = A section to apply `consume effects` [Optional].

syntax_12912.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12912.title: PotionType of Item
syntax_12912.description:
Get/set/delete the potion type of an item.
This is not the same as the potion effect type, this is the base potion Minecraft uses for the potion items in the creative menu.

syntax_12913.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12913.title: ItemComponent - Potion Contents Component Apply
syntax_12913.description:
Apply a potion contents component to an item (can be used on a potion/arrow or any consumable item).
See [**Potion Contents Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#potion_contents) on McWiki for more info.
Note: `potion` and `custom_effects` entries cannot be used together.

**Entries**:
- `potion` = The base potion of the item.
- `custom_color` = The overriding color of this potion texture, and/or the particles of the area effect cloud created.
- `custom_name` = An optional string used to generate containing stack name. (See McWiki for more details on this) [Optional]
- `custom_effects` = A list of the additional effects that this item should apply. [Optional]
syntax_12913.entries: [{"name": "potion", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "custom_color", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "custom_name", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "custom_effects", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}]

en:
Apply a potion contents component to an item (can be used on a potion/arrow or any consumable item).
See [**Potion Contents Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#potion_contents) on McWiki for more info.
Note: `potion` and `custom_effects` entries cannot be used together.

**Entries**:
- `potion` = The base potion of the item.
- `custom_color` = The overriding color of this potion texture, and/or the particles of the area effect cloud created.
- `custom_name` = An optional string used to generate containing stack name. (See McWiki for more details on this) [Optional]
- `custom_effects` = A list of the additional effects that this item should apply. [Optional]

syntax_11821.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11821.title: ItemComponent - Food Component Apply
syntax_11821.description:
Apply a food component to any item giving it food properties.
You will also require a `consumable` component to actually make the item consumable.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Food Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#food) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `nutrition` = The number of food points restored by this item when eaten. Must be a non-negative integer.
- `saturation` = The amount of saturation restored by this item when eaten.
- `can always eat` = If true, this item can be eaten even if the player is not hungry. Defaults to false. [Optional]
syntax_11821.entries: [{"name": "nutrition", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "saturation", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "can_always_eat", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply a food component to any item giving it food properties.
You will also require a `consumable` component to actually make the item consumable.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Food Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#food) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `nutrition` = The number of food points restored by this item when eaten. Must be a non-negative integer.
- `saturation` = The amount of saturation restored by this item when eaten.
- `can always eat` = If true, this item can be eaten even if the player is not hungry. Defaults to false. [Optional]

syntax_12896.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12896.title: ItemComponent - Equippable Component Apply
syntax_12896.description:
Apply an equippable component to any item so it can be equipped in the specified slot.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Equippable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#equippable) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `slot` = The slot the item can be put on (See Equipment Slot).
- `equip_sound` = The sound to be played when equipped. [Optional]
- `asset_id` = The key of the equipment model to use when equipped. [Optional]
- `allowed_entities` = A list of entity types or a Minecraft entity tag that can equip this item. [Optional]
- `dispensable` = Whether the item can be dispensed by using a dispenser. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `swappable` = Whether the item can be equipped into the relevant slot by right-clicking. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `damage_on_hurt` = Whether this item is damaged when the wearing entity is damaged. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `camera_overlay` = The key of the overlay texture to use when equipped. [Optional]
syntax_12896.entries: [{"name": "slot", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "equip_sound", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "asset_id", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "allowed_entities", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "dispensable", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "swappable", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "damage_on_hurt", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "camera_overlay", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply an equippable component to any item so it can be equipped in the specified slot.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Equippable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#equippable) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `slot` = The slot the item can be put on (See Equipment Slot).
- `equip_sound` = The sound to be played when equipped. [Optional]
- `asset_id` = The key of the equipment model to use when equipped. [Optional]
- `allowed_entities` = A list of entity types or a Minecraft entity tag that can equip this item. [Optional]
- `dispensable` = Whether the item can be dispensed by using a dispenser. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `swappable` = Whether the item can be equipped into the relevant slot by right-clicking. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `damage_on_hurt` = Whether this item is damaged when the wearing entity is damaged. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `camera_overlay` = The key of the overlay texture to use when equipped. [Optional]

syntax_11370.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_11370.title: Player Attempt Item Pickup
syntax_11370.description:
Called when a player attempts to pick an item up from the ground.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-remaining`
   - **Description**: Represents the amount that will remain on the ground, if any.
 - `past event-number`
   - **Description**: Represents the item amount of the dropped item before pickup.
 - `event-dropped item`
   - **Description**: Represents the dropped item entity that is attempting to pickup.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
syntax_11370.event_values: event-dropped item, event-player, event-remaining, event-world, past event-number

en:
Called when a player attempts to pick an item up from the ground.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-remaining`
   - **Description**: Represents the amount that will remain on the ground, if any.
 - `past event-number`
   - **Description**: Represents the item amount of the dropped item before pickup.
 - `event-dropped item`
   - **Description**: Represents the dropped item entity that is attempting to pickup.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`

syntax_10460.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10460.title: Player Chunk Load
syntax_10460.description:
Is called when a Player receives a Chunk.
Can for example be used for spawning a fake entity when the player receives a chunk. 
Should only be used for packet/clientside related stuff. Not intended for modifying server side state.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-chunk`

en:
Is called when a Player receives a Chunk.
Can for example be used for spawning a fake entity when the player receives a chunk. 
Should only be used for packet/clientside related stuff. Not intended for modifying server side state.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-chunk`

syntax_10464.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10464.title: Player Leash
syntax_10464.description:
Called immediately prior to a creature being leashed by a player.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-entity`
   - **Description**: Entity which got leashed.
 - `event-player`
   - **Description**: Player whom leashed the entity.
 - `future event-entity`
   - **Description**: The entity the leashed entity is leashed to (could be a player or leash hitch on a fence).

syntax_10473.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10473.title: Shear Entity
syntax_10473.description:
Called when a player shears an entity.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-entity`
   - **Description**: The entity that is getting sheared.
 - `event-item stack`
   - **Description**: The item that was used to shear the entity.
 - `event-item type`
   - **Description**: The item that was used to shear the entity.
 - `event-equipment slot`
   - **Description**: The hand the player used to shear.
 - `event-item stacks`
   - **Description**: Get a list of drops for this shearing.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-item types`
   - **Description**: Get a list of drops for this shearing.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
syntax_10473.event_values: event-entity, event-equipment slot, event-item stack, event-item stacks, event-item type, event-item types, event-player, event-world

syntax_14863.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14863.title: World GameRule Change
syntax_14863.description:
Called when a gamerule is changed in a world.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-value`
   - **Description**: Gets the new value of the gamerule (Why its a string, I dunno!).
   - **Patterns**: `event-value`, `event-changed-value`
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-gamerule value`
   - **Description**: Gets the new value of the gamerule as a Skript GameRuleValue.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-gamerule`
   - **Description**: Gets the gamerule that was changed.
 - `event-command sender`
   - **Description**: Gets the command sender associated with this event (null if changed via code).
 - `event-world`
syntax_14863.event_values: event-command sender, event-gamerule, event-gamerule value, event-value, event-world

en:
Called when a gamerule is changed in a world.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-value`
   - **Description**: Gets the new value of the gamerule (Why its a string, I dunno!).

   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-gamerule value`
   - **Description**: Gets the new value of the gamerule as a Skript GameRuleValue.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-gamerule`
   - **Description**: Gets the gamerule that was changed.
 - `event-command sender`
   - **Description**: Gets the command sender associated with this event (null if changed via code).
 - `event-world`

syntax_10443.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10443.title: Damage By Block
syntax_10443.description:
Called when an entity is damaged by a block.
Anything that works in vanilla Skript's damage event (victim/damage cause/damage/final damage)
will all work in this event too.

`victim` = Same as vanilla Skript, `victim` is used to get the damaged entity.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-block`
   - **Description**: The block that damaged the entity.
 - `event-damage source`
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
 - `event-damage cause`
syntax_10443.event_values: event-block, event-command sender, event-damage cause, event-damage source, event-entity, event-entity type, event-location, event-world

will all work in this event too.

**Event Values:**
 - `event-block`
   - **Description**: The block that damaged the entity.

 - `event-entity`

 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
 - `event-damage cause`

syntax_10462.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10462.title: Player Click Unknown Entity
syntax_10462.description:
Represents an event that is called when a player right-clicks an unknown entity.
Useful for dealing with virtual entities (entities that aren't actually spawned on the server).
This event may be called multiple times per interaction (this is a server issue).
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-number`
   - **Description**: Returns the entity id of the unknown entity that was interacted with (Not sure if this usefull or not).
 - `event-vector`
   - **Description**: Returns the position relative to the entity that was clicked if available.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
syntax_10462.syntax_pattern:
[on] [player] click unknown entity [with (main|off) hand]
[on] [player] right[( |-)]click unknown entity [with (main|off) hand]
[on] [player] left[( |-)]click unknown entity [with (main|off) hand]

en:
Represents an event that is called when a player right-clicks an unknown entity.
Useful for dealing with virtual entities (entities that aren't actually spawned on the server).
This event may be called multiple times per interaction (this is a server issue).
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-number`
   - **Description**: Returns the entity id of the unknown entity that was interacted with (Not sure if this usefull or not).
 - `event-vector`
   - **Description**: Returns the position relative to the entity that was clicked if available.
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`

syntax_10468.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10468.title: Pre Creature Spawn
syntax_10468.description:
Called before an entity is spawned into the world.
Note: The spawning entity does not exist when this event is called only the entitytype exists.
This event is called very frequently, and can cause server lag, use it sparingly.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-location`
   - **Description**: The location the spawned entity will appear.
 - `event-entity type`
   - **Description**: The type of entity being spawned.
 - `event-minecraft entity type`
   - **Description**: The Minecraft EntityType being spawned.
 - `event-spawn reason`
   - **Description**: The reason the entity is spawned.
syntax_10468.event_values: event-entity type, event-location, event-minecraft entity type, event-spawn reason

en:
Called before an entity is spawned into the world.
Note: The spawning entity does not exist when this event is called only the entitytype exists.
This event is called very frequently, and can cause server lag, use it sparingly.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-location`
   - **Description**: The location the spawned entity will appear.
 - `event-entity type`
   - **Description**: The type of entity being spawned.
 - `event-minecraft entity type`
   - **Description**: The Minecraft EntityType being spawned.
 - `event-spawn reason`
   - **Description**: The reason the entity is spawned.

syntax_10471.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10471.title: Recipe - Discover Event
syntax_10471.description:
Called when a player unlocks a recipe.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
   - **Description**: The recipe NamespacedKey as a string (this will also include either "minecraft:" or "mykeyhere:").
 - `event-namespacedkey`

 - `event-boolean (yes/no)`
   - **Description**: Whether or not to show a notification (toast) to the player.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`

en:
Called when a player unlocks a recipe.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
   - **Description**: The recipe NamespacedKey as a string (this will also include either "minecraft:" or "mykeyhere:").
 - `event-namespacedkey`
   - **Description**: The recipe NamespacedKey (this will also include either "minecraft:" or "mykeyhere:").
 - `event-boolean (yes/no)`
   - **Description**: Whether or not to show a notification (toast) to the player.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`

syntax_13170.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13170.title: FastBoard - Clear
syntax_13170.description:
Clear a fastboard of a player.
NOTE: You do NOT need to clear a fastboard before changing lines.

en:
Clear a fastboard of a player.
NOTE: You do NOT need to clear a fastboard before changing lines.

syntax_13174.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13174.title: FastBoard - Line
syntax_13174.description:
Get/set/delete lines of a player's fastboard.
Lines are valid from 1 to 15. 1 being the line at the top and 15 being the bottom (This can be changed in the config).
Line length is unlimited.
Supports number format on Minecraft 1.20.4+ by providing 2 strings (see examples).
When running Paper, text components are supported.

en:
Get/set/delete lines of a player's fastboard.
Lines are valid from 1 to 15. 1 being the line at the top and 15 being the bottom (This can be changed in the config).
Line length is unlimited.
Supports number format on Minecraft 1.20.4+ by providing 2 strings (see examples).
When running Paper, text components are supported.

syntax_13175.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13175.title: FastBoard - Title
syntax_13175.description: Get/set the title of a player's fastboard.

en: Get/set the title of a player's fastboard.

syntax_13172.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13172.title: ChunkGenerator - Biome Parameter Point
syntax_13172.description:
Represents the biome noise parameters which may be passed to a world generator.
This is used in the `biome gen` section of a chunk generator.

en:
Represents the biome noise parameters which may be passed to a world generator.
This is used in the `biome gen` section of a chunk generator.

syntax_13169.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13169.title: FastBoard - Is On
syntax_13169.description: Check if a player's fastboard is currently toggled on/off.
syntax_13169.syntax_pattern:
[score|fast]board of %player% is (on|true)
[score|fast]board of %player% is(n't| not) on
[score|fast]board of %player% is (off|false)

en: Check if a player's fastboard is currently toggled on/off.

syntax_13171.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13171.title: FastBoard - Toggle
syntax_13171.description: Toggle a fastboard on or off.

en: Toggle a fastboard on or off.

syntax_11815.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11815.title: ItemComponent - Clear/Reset Components
syntax_11815.description:
Clear/reset data components of an item. Requires Minecraft 1.21+
- `clear/remove/unset` = Will remove the component from the item.
- `reset` = Will reset the data component of the item back to its default value.

en:
Clear/reset data components of an item. Requires Minecraft 1.21+
- `clear/remove/unset` = Will remove the component from the item.
- `reset` = Will reset the data component of the item back to its default value.

syntax_12864.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12864.title: ItemComponent - Charged Projectiles
syntax_12864.description:
The items loaded as projectiles into a crossbow. If not present, the crossbow is not charged.
See [**Charged Projectiles Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#charged_projectiles) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+

**Changers**:
- `set` = Set the items to be loaded by the crossbow.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

en:
The items loaded as projectiles into a crossbow. If not present, the crossbow is not charged.
See [**Charged Projectiles Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#charged_projectiles) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+

**Changers**:
- `set` = Set the items to be loaded by the crossbow.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

syntax_13176.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13176.title: ItemComponent - Dyed Color
syntax_13176.description:
Represents the dyed color component of an item.
This will work on leather armor, or items that have a dye component on their item model.
See [**ItemModel**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#dyed_color) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+

**Changers**:
`set` = Will set the dyed color of the item.
`delete` = Will delete the dyed color of this item.
`reset` = Will reset the dyed color back to the original value.

en:
Represents the dyed color component of an item.
This will work on leather armor, or items that have a dye component on their item model.
See [**ItemModel**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#dyed_color) on McWiki for more details.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+

**Changers**:
`set` = Will set the dyed color of the item.
`delete` = Will delete the dyed color of this item.
`reset` = Will reset the dyed color back to the original value.

syntax_11938.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11938.title: Recipe - Crafter Craft Event
syntax_11938.description:
Called when a Crafter is about to craft an item. Requires Minecraft 1.21.1+
`event-string` = The key for the recipe used in this event.
`recipe result` = An expression that reprsents the result slot (can be changed).
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
 - `event-block`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`

Called when a Crafter is about to craft an item. Requires Minecraft 1.21.1+
`event-string` = The key for the recipe used in this event.
`recipe result` = An expression that reprsents the result slot (can be changed).
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
 - `event-block`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`

syntax_14864.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14864.title: Entity Would Collide With
syntax_14864.description:
Check if an entity would collide with another entity or block.
Optionally you can check if an entity would collide with anything at a location.

en:
Check if an entity would collide with another entity or block.
Optionally you can check if an entity would collide with anything at a location.

syntax_10732.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10732.title: Scoreboard - Objective DisplaySlot
syntax_10732.description: Get/Set the display slot of an objective.

en: Get/Set the display slot of an objective.

syntax_13179.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13179.title: Scoreboard - Objective from DisplaySlot
syntax_13179.description: Get/clear objectives from specific display slots of scoreboards.

en: Get/clear objectives from specific display slots of scoreboards.

syntax_13182.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13182.title: TextComponent - Item Lore Lines
syntax_13182.description: Get/set/delete specific lines of lore of an item using text components.
syntax_13182.syntax_pattern:
line %integer% of [the] component [item] lore of %itemstack/itemtype/slot%
%itemstack/itemtype/slot%'[s] line %integer% of component [item] lore

en: Get/set/delete specific lines of lore of an item using text components.

syntax_13180.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13180.title: Scoreboard - Scoreboard Get
syntax_13180.description: Get the vanilla scoreboard, or create a new scoreboard (custom scoreboard are not persistent).

en: Get the vanilla scoreboard, or create a new scoreboard (custom scoreboard are not persistent).

syntax_13181.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13181.title: Scoreboard - Scoreboard of Player
syntax_13181.description:
Get/set the scoreboard of players.
By default all players share the same main scoreboard unless given a new scoreboard.
Do note that custom scoreboards are not persistent.
Reset will reset the player's scoreboard back to the main server scoreboard.

en:
Get/set the scoreboard of players.
By default all players share the same main scoreboard unless given a new scoreboard.
Do note that custom scoreboards are not persistent.
Reset will reset the player's scoreboard back to the main server scoreboard.

syntax_10762.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10762.title: Team - From Entity
syntax_10762.description:
Get an instance of a team from an entity.
You have the option to get a team from a specific scoreboard (defaults to the main scoreboard).
Teams off the main scoreboard cannot be serialized/saved to variables (This is because custom scoreboards aren't persistent).

en:
Get an instance of a team from an entity.
You have the option to get a team from a specific scoreboard (defaults to the main scoreboard).
Teams off the main scoreboard cannot be serialized/saved to variables (This is because custom scoreboards aren't persistent).

syntax_10731.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10731.title: Scoreboard - Objective Create
syntax_10731.description:
Create an objective.
- `id` = The id used to keep track of your objective.
- `criteria` = Uses a criteria object. If using an older server version, this will just be a string.
- `named` = The display name of this objective.
- `for scoreboard` = Optionally use a specific scoreboard (will default to the main scoreboard).
- `render type` = How this objective will render, hearts or integer.
- `display slot` = Where this objective will render.
syntax_10731.syntax_pattern: objective with id %string% with [criteria] %criteria/string% named %string% [(for|of|from) %scoreboard%] [with render[ ]type %-rendertype%] [(with|in) [display[ ]slot] %-displayslot%]

en:
Create an objective.
- `id` = The id used to keep track of your objective.
- `criteria` = Uses a criteria object. If using an older server version, this will just be a string.
- `named` = The display name of this objective.
- `for scoreboard` = Optionally use a specific scoreboard (will default to the main scoreboard).
- `render type` = How this objective will render, hearts or integer.
- `display slot` = Where this objective will render.

syntax_13177.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13177.title: Scoreboard - Objective Score Custom Name
syntax_13177.description:
Get/change the custom name of the score of an objective for an entry.
Supports both components and strings.
- `[component]` = Will return as a text component (for set this doesn't matter).
- `delete` = Will set the name to an empty component.
- `reset` = Will reset back to the original name.

en:
Get/change the custom name of the score of an objective for an entry.
Supports both components and strings.
- `[component]` = Will return as a text component (for set this doesn't matter).
- `delete` = Will set the name to an empty component.
- `reset` = Will reset back to the original name.

syntax_12869.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12869.title: ItemComponent - Repairable
syntax_12869.description:
Represents the items/tags that will be used to repair an item in an anvil.
See [**Repairable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#repairable) on McWiki for more details.

**Patterns**:
`repairable items` = A list of items that are used.
`repairable tag` = A single Minecraft item tag that is used.

**Changers**:
- `set` = Set the items/tag to allow for repairing.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).
syntax_12869.syntax_pattern:
repairable [component] items of %itemstacks/itemtypes/slots%
repairable [component] tag of %itemstacks/slots%

en:
Represents the items/tags that will be used to repair an item in an anvil.
See [**Repairable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#repairable) on McWiki for more details.

**Patterns**:
`repairable items` = A list of items that are used.
`repairable tag` = A single Minecraft item tag that is used.

**Changers**:
- `set` = Set the items/tag to allow for repairing.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

syntax_10734.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10734.title: Scoreboard - Objective Name
syntax_10734.description:
Represents the name/display name of an objective.
- `name` = The name/id given to the objective (Cannot be changed).
- `display name` = The name the players will see [as a string] (Can be changed).
- `component display name` = The name the players will see [as a text component] (Can be changed).
syntax_10734.syntax_pattern:
objective (name|id) of %objective%
objective [component] display name of %objective%

en:
Represents the name/display name of an objective.
- `name` = The name/id given to the objective (Cannot be changed).
- `display name` = The name the players will see [as a string] (Can be changed).
- `component display name` = The name the players will see [as a text component] (Can be changed).

syntax_13183.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13183.title: ChunkGenerator - Biome Parameter Point
syntax_13183.description:
The parameter points of a biome during chunk generation.
See [**World Generation/Biomes**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/World_generation#Biomes) on McWiki for more details.

en:
The parameter points of a biome during chunk generation.
See [**World Generation/Biomes**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/World_generation#Biomes) on McWiki for more details.

syntax_13184.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13184.title: Scoreboard
syntax_13184.description:
Represents the vanilla scoreboard of the server/players.
This can be the main server scoreboard, or a custom scoreboard.
Do note custom scoreboards are not persistent (do not save to the server).
Multiple players can share a custom scoreboard.
See [**Scoreboard**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Scoreboard) on McWiki for more info.

en:
Represents the vanilla scoreboard of the server/players.
This can be the main server scoreboard, or a custom scoreboard.
Do note custom scoreboards are not persistent (do not save to the server).
Multiple players can share a custom scoreboard.
See [**Scoreboard**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Scoreboard) on McWiki for more info.
Create a new Timespan.

syntax_13186.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13186.title: ChunkGenerator - Peaks and Valleys
syntax_13186.description: Peaks and Valleys in chunk generation.

en: Peaks and Valleys in chunk generation.

syntax_13185.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_13185.title: Time Period
syntax_13185.description: Represents the time periods of a Timespan.
syntax_13185.type_usage: day, hour, millisecond, minute, month, second, tick, week, year

en: Represents the time periods of a Timespan.

syntax_13189.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13189.title: Tag Aliases
syntax_13189.description:
Create item/block aliases that use Minecraft tags.
Supports paper and datapack tags as well.
Custom Skript tags will not work here as they're registered after this structure loads.

**Global vs Local**:
Global will add your aliases to Skript's aliases and can be used in any script/effect commands.
Local will add your aliases to the aliases of that specific script.
Will default to local if not specified.

en:
Create item/block aliases that use Minecraft tags.
Supports paper and datapack tags as well.
Custom Skript tags will not work here as they're registered after this structure loads.

**Global vs Local**:
Global will add your aliases to Skript's aliases and can be used in any script/effect commands.
Local will add your aliases to the aliases of that specific script.
Will default to local if not specified.

syntax_12894.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12894.title: ItemComponent - CustomModelData Component Apply
syntax_12894.description:
Apply a custom model data component to items.
Requires Paper 1.21.4+
See [**CustomModelData Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#custom_model_data) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `floats` = A list of numbers.
- `flags` = A list of booleans (true/false).
- `strings` = A list of strings.
- `colors` = A list of colors.
syntax_12894.entries: [{"name": "floats", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "flags", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "strings", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "colors", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply a custom model data component to items.
Requires Paper 1.21.4+
See [**CustomModelData Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#custom_model_data) on McWiki for more info.

**Entries**:
- `floats` = A list of numbers.
- `flags` = A list of booleans (true/false).
- `strings` = A list of strings.
- `colors` = A list of colors.

syntax_13237.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13237.title: Property
syntax_13237.description:
Represents the diffrent kinds of properties.
See [**Property Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBee/wiki/Properties) for available properties.

en:
Represents the diffrent kinds of properties.
See [**Property Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBee/wiki/Properties) for available properties.

syntax_14865.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14865.title: ResourcePack - Status - Intermediate
syntax_14865.description: Whether, after receiving this status, further status events might occur.

en: Whether, after receiving this status, further status events might occur.

syntax_14866.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14866.title: ResourcePack - Apply Pack
syntax_14866.description:
Apply a resource pack in the `packs` section of a resouce pack send section.
The ID is used to remove the pack later, if you dont plan on removing a pack you can use a `random uuid`.
syntax_14866.syntax_pattern: apply [resource] pack with url %string% [and] with (uuid|id) %uuid% [and with hash %-string%]

en:
Apply a resource pack in the `packs` section of a resouce pack send section.
The ID is used to remove the pack later, if you dont plan on removing a pack you can use a `random uuid`.

syntax_13280.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13280.title: Dispatch Command
syntax_13280.description:
Similar to Skript's command effect, with the option to use any command sender
(vs. being restricted to only player/console), and the option to temporarily attach a permission.
The attached permission will only last for 1 tick, and automatically remove. This is not persistent.

en:
Similar to Skript's command effect, with the option to use any command sender
(vs. being restricted to only player/console), and the option to temporarily attach a permission.
The attached permission will only last for 1 tick, and automatically remove. This is not persistent.

syntax_13279.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13279.title: Folia - Object Owned by Current Region
syntax_13279.description:
Returns whether the current thread is ticking a region and that the region being ticked owns the entity/block/location/chunk at the specified world/position as included in the specified object.
The chunk pattern accepts Chunk X/Z, not world position.
This is useful to check before manipulating entities/blocks/ect which may not be in the same region as the caller.
syntax_13279.syntax_pattern:
%entities/blocks/locations% (is|are)[n't] owned by current region
chunk at %integer%,[ ]%integer% (in|of) %world% is[n't] owned by current region

en:
Returns whether the current thread is ticking a region and that the region being ticked owns the entity/block/location/chunk at the specified world/position as included in the specified object.
The chunk pattern accepts Chunk X/Z, not world position.
This is useful to check before manipulating entities/blocks/ect which may not be in the same region as the caller.

syntax_13277.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13277.title: Tick Start Event
syntax_13277.description:
Called each time the server starts its main tick loop.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-tick-number`
   - **Description**: The current tick number.

en:
Called each time the server starts its main tick loop.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-tick-number`
   - **Description**: The current tick number.

syntax_13278.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13278.title: Unknown Command
syntax_13278.description:
This event is fired when a player executes a command that is not defined.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
   - **Description**: The command that was sent.
 - `event-command sender`
   - **Description**: Who sent the command.
 - `event-message`
   - **Description**: The message that will be returned.

en:
This event is fired when a player executes a command that is not defined.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
   - **Description**: The command that was sent.
 - `event-command sender`
   - **Description**: Who sent the command.
 - `event-message`
   - **Description**: The message that will be returned.

syntax_13188.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13188.title: Repeating While Loop
syntax_13188.description:
Similar to Skript's while loop, this while loop will repeat at the given timespan.
It is recommended to NOT use a wait within these sections, as the section will repeat regardless.

**Patterns**:
The 2nd pattern is only of concern if you are running Folia or have Paper schedulers enabled in the config, otherwise just use the first pattern.
- `globally` = Will run this loop on the global scheduler (Use this for non entity/block related tasks).
- `for %entity` = Will run this task for an entity, will follow the entity around (region wise)and will cancel itself when the entity is no longer valid.
- `at %location%` = Will run this loop at a specific location (Use this for block related tasks).
syntax_13188.syntax_pattern:
while <.+> repeating every %timespan% [globally]
while <.+> repeating every %timespan% [(at|on|for) %-entity/location%]

en:
Similar to Skript's while loop, this while loop will repeat at the given timespan.
It is recommended to NOT use a wait within these sections, as the section will repeat regardless.

**Patterns**:
The 2nd pattern is only of concern if you are running Folia or have Paper schedulers enabled in the config, otherwise just use the first pattern.
- `globally` = Will run this loop on the global scheduler (Use this for non entity/block related tasks).
- `for %entity` = Will run this task for an entity, will follow the entity around (region wise)and will cancel itself when the entity is no longer valid.
- `at %location%` = Will run this loop at a specific location (Use this for block related tasks).

syntax_14867.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14867.title: Entity Collision
syntax_14867.description:
Get/set whether this entity will be subject to collisions with other entities.
Note that the client may predict the collision between itself and another entity, resulting in this flag not working for player collisions.
This expression should therefore only be used to set the collision status of non-player entities.
Resetting will set the collision status to true.

en:
Get/set whether this entity will be subject to collisions with other entities.
Note that the client may predict the collision between itself and another entity, resulting in this flag not working for player collisions.
This expression should therefore only be used to set the collision status of non-player entities.
Resetting will set the collision status to true.

syntax_14868.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14868.title: Player List Order
syntax_14868.description: Get/set the relative order that the player is shown on the in-game player list.
syntax_14868.syntax_pattern:
[the] player list order of %players%
%players%'[s] player list order

en: Get/set the relative order that the player is shown on the in-game player list.

syntax_13236.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13236.title: Property - Property of Object
syntax_13236.description:
Represents different properties of an object.
See [**Property Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBee/wiki/Properties) for available properties and examples.

en:
Represents different properties of an object.
See [**Property Wiki**](https://github.com/ShaneBeee/SkBee/wiki/Properties) for available properties and examples.

syntax_13305.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13305.title: TextComponent - Block Name
syntax_13305.description:
Get/set/delete the custom name of a block. This will work on container blocks and skulls.
Even though the custom name of any skull can be set, only a player head will retain its name when broken (Skeleton/creeper/etc skulls will not).
syntax_13305.syntax_pattern:
[the] component [custom] block name of %block%
%block%'[s] component [custom] block name

en:
Get/set/delete the custom name of a block. This will work on container blocks and skulls.
Even though the custom name of any skull can be set, only a player head will retain its name when broken (Skeleton/creeper/etc skulls will not).

syntax_14869.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14869.title: Player - Shoulder Entity
syntax_14869.description:
Gets the entity currently perched on the shoulder of a player or null if no entity.
The returned entity will not be spawned within the world, so most operations are invalid unless the entity is first spawned in.
When setting, this will remove the entity from the world.
Note that only a copy of the entity will be set to display on the shoulder.
Also note that the client will currently only render Parrot entities.
Deleting will remove the entity from the player's shoulder.
Resetting will release the entity from the player's shoulder.

en:
Gets the entity currently perched on the shoulder of a player or null if no entity.
The returned entity will not be spawned within the world, so most operations are invalid unless the entity is first spawned in.
When setting, this will remove the entity from the world.
Note that only a copy of the entity will be set to display on the shoulder.
Also note that the client will currently only render Parrot entities.
Deleting will remove the entity from the player's shoulder.
Resetting will release the entity from the player's shoulder.

syntax_14699.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14699.title: Entity Use Item
syntax_14699.description:
Makes a LivingEntity start/stop/complete using an item.
You can optionally specify which hand. Will default to main hand.
**Types**:
 - Start = Makes the entity start using an item.
 - Stop = Interrupts any ongoing active "usage" or consumption or an item.
 - Complete = Finishes using the currently active item.
   When, for example, a skeleton is drawing its bow, this will cause it to release and fire the arrow.This method does not make any guarantees about the effect of this method as such depends on the entity and its state.
syntax_14699.syntax_pattern:
make %livingentities% start using item [in %-equipmentslot%]
make %livingentities% stop using active item
make %livingentities% complete using active item

en:
Makes a LivingEntity start/stop/complete using an item.
You can optionally specify which hand. Will default to main hand.
**Types**:
 - Start = Makes the entity start using an item.
 - Stop = Interrupts any ongoing active "usage" or consumption or an item.
 - Complete = Finishes using the currently active item.
   When, for example, a skeleton is drawing its bow, this will cause it to release and fire the arrow.This method does not make any guarantees about the effect of this method as such depends on the entity and its state.

syntax_14700.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14700.title: BlockFace - Direction
syntax_14700.description:
Gets a skript direction using a blockface with an optional distance value.
Not defining a distance will default it to 0.
**NOTE:** this will not work using the `self_face` block face, as Skript makes it NaN.

en:
Gets a skript direction using a blockface with an optional distance value.
Not defining a distance will default it to 0.
**NOTE:** this will not work using the `self_face` block face, as Skript makes it NaN.

syntax_14701.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14701.title: Parse Condition - With Return
syntax_14701.description:
This will parse a string as a condition and then check it and returns whether or not it's valid.
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

**NOTE:** This is handled very differently from the parse effect expression in addition to behaving differently than Skript's `whether <condition>`.
If you have no good reason to use this, please check out the other two.
*tip: when running into invalid null states try replacing parts with variables*

en:
This will parse a string as a condition and then check it and returns whether or not it's valid.
If you provide a command sender it works the same as Skript's 'effect commands'.
Otherwise it runs using the current event allowing you to use event-values

**NOTE:** This is handled very differently from the parse effect expression in addition to behaving differently than Skript's `whether <condition>`.
If you have no good reason to use this, please check out the other two.
*tip: when running into invalid null states try replacing parts with variables*

syntax_10602.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10602.title: Bound - Coords
syntax_10602.description:
DEPRECATED - Use bound locations/world expressions instead
The coords and world of a bounding box. You can get the world/coords for a specific bound, you can also set the coords of a bounding box. You can NOT set the world of a bounding box. 
Greater will always equal the higher south-east corner. 
Lesser will always equal the lower north-west corner.
syntax_10602.syntax_pattern:
lesser (x|y|z) coord[inate] of %bounds%
greater (x|y|z) coord[inate] of %bounds%
world of bound %bounds%

en:
DEPRECATED - Use bound locations/world expressions instead
The coords and world of a bounding box. You can get the world/coords for a specific bound, you can also set the coords of a bounding box. You can NOT set the world of a bounding box. 
Greater will always equal the higher south-east corner. 
Lesser will always equal the lower north-west corner.

syntax_14506.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14506.title: ItemComponent - Damage Resistant
syntax_14506.description:
Represents the damage resistant component of an item.
**Requires Minecraft 26.1+**
If specified, this item is invulnerable to the specified damage types when in entity form or equipped.
See [**Damage Resistant**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#damage_resistant) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
`set` = Will set the damage resistant component of the item (Accepts a DamageType TagKey or a list of DamageTypes).
`delete` = Will delete the damage resistant component of this item.
`reset` = Will reset the damage resistant component back to the original value.

en:
Represents the damage resistant component of an item.
**Requires Minecraft 26.1+**
If specified, this item is invulnerable to the specified damage types when in entity form or equipped.

**Changers**:
`set` = Will set the damage resistant component of the item (Accepts a DamageType TagKey or a list of DamageTypes).
`delete` = Will delete the damage resistant component of this item.
`reset` = Will reset the damage resistant component back to the original value.

syntax_10773.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10773.title: TextComponent - Format
syntax_10773.description:
Change formatting options of text components. Most of these are pretty straight forward. 
Insertion means the text that will copy to chat when a player shift-clicks the component (Might not be available on all versions). 
Color supports color names as well as RGB color codes via Skript's RGB function (see examples).
Fallback is the fallback text used in a translation component when the client cannot find said translation.

en:
Change formatting options of text components. Most of these are pretty straight forward. 
Insertion means the text that will copy to chat when a player shift-clicks the component (Might not be available on all versions). 
Color supports color names as well as RGB color codes via Skript's RGB function (see examples).
Fallback is the fallback text used in a translation component when the client cannot find said translation.

syntax_13413.title: World Time
syntax_13413.description:
Get/set the time of world, represented as an integer.
`world time` = The 24000 tick day cycle of a world.
`full world time` = The time of a world over all days.

syntax_14870.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14870.title: World Spawn Limit
syntax_14870.description:
Get/set the spawn limit of a world.
If the world is ommited, the server spawn limit will be returned.
World spawn limits can be changed, server spawn limits cannot.
If set to a negative number the world will use the server-wide spawn limit instead, reset will do the same.
You can read more about [**Mob Caps**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Mob_spawning#Java_Edition_mob_cap) on McWiki
Note: The `misc` category is not supported by this expression.
Note: Changes are not persistent thru server restarts.
syntax_14870.syntax_pattern:
[the] server wide %spawncategory% spawn limit
[the] %spawncategory% spawn limit[s] [of %-worlds%]

en:
Get/set the spawn limit of a world.
If the world is ommited, the server spawn limit will be returned.
World spawn limits can be changed, server spawn limits cannot.
If set to a negative number the world will use the server-wide spawn limit instead, reset will do the same.
You can read more about [**Mob Caps**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Mob_spawning#Java_Edition_mob_cap) on McWiki
Note: The `misc` category is not supported by this expression.
Note: Changes are not persistent thru server restarts.

syntax_10447.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10447.title: Entity Change Block
syntax_10447.description:
Called when any Entity changes a block and a more specific event is not available.
Skript does partially have this event, but this version of it opens up ALL possibilities with this event.

event-entity = the entity which changed the block

event-block = the block that changed

event-blockdata = the blockdata the block has changed into
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-block data`
 - `past event-block`
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
 - `event-block`
 - `future event-block data`

syntax_10454.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10454.title: Entity Unleash
syntax_10454.description:
Called immediately prior to an entity being unleashed.
Cancelling this event when either the leashed entity dies, the entity changes dimension, or
the client has disconnected the leash will have no effect.

`event-string` = The reason for unleashing.
Options are "distance" (When the entity's leashholder is more than 10 blocks away),
"holder_gone" (When the entity's leashholder has died or logged out, and so is unleashed),
"player_unleash" (When the entity's leashholder attempts to unleash it), "unknown"
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-text`
 - `event-player`
 - `event-unleash reason`
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
syntax_10454.event_values: event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity type, event-location, event-player, event-text, event-unleash reason, event-world

"holder_gone" (When the entity's leashholder has died or logged out, and so is unleashed),
"player_unleash" (When the entity's leashholder attempts to unleash it), "unknown"
 
**Event Values:**

 - `event-entity`

 - `event-location`

syntax_13276.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13276.title: Tick End Event
syntax_13276.description:
Called when the server has finished ticking the main loop.
There may be time left after this event is called, and before the next tick starts.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-tick-number`
   - **Description**: The current tick number.
 - `event-tick-duration`
   - **Description**: Time of how long this tick took.
 - `event-tick-remaining`
   - **Description**: Amount of time remaining before the next tick should start.

en:

   - **Description**: Time of how long this tick took.
 - `event-tick-remaining`
   - **Description**: Amount of time remaining before the next tick should start.

 - `event-entity type`

syntax_10450.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_10450.title: On Entity Potion Effect
syntax_10450.description:
Called when a potion effect is modified on an entity.
ADDED = When the potion effect is added because the entity didn't have it's type.
CHANGED = When the entity already had the potion effect type, but the effect is changed.
CLEARED = When the effect is removed due to all effects being removed.
REMOVED = When the potion effect type is completely removed.
event-potioneffect = new effect.
past event-potioneffect = old effect.
event-potioneffecttype = type of potion effect.
syntax_10450.syntax_pattern:
[on] [entity] potion effect added
[on] [entity] potion effect changed
[on] [entity] potion effect cleared
[on] [entity] potion effect removed
syntax_10450.event_values: event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity potion cause, event-entity type, event-location, event-potion effect, event-potion effect type, event-world, past event-potion effect

en:
Called when a potion effect is modified on an entity.
ADDED = When the potion effect is added because the entity didn't have it's type.
CHANGED = When the entity already had the potion effect type, but the effect is changed.
CLEARED = When the effect is removed due to all effects being removed.
REMOVED = When the potion effect type is completely removed.
event-potioneffect = new effect.
past event-potioneffect = old effect.
event-potioneffecttype = type of potion effect.

syntax_11922.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11922.title: ItemComponent - Enchantment Glint Override
syntax_11922.description:
Represents the enchantment glint override of an item. 
Overrides the enchantment glint effect on an item.
When `true`, the item will display a glint, even without enchantments.
When `false`, the item will not display a glint, even with enchantments.
**Note**: If no override is applied, will return null.
See [**EnchantmentGlintOverride**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#enchantment_glint_override) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = Allows you to override the glint.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

en:
Represents the enchantment glint override of an item. 
Overrides the enchantment glint effect on an item.
When `true`, the item will display a glint, even without enchantments.
When `false`, the item will not display a glint, even with enchantments.
**Note**: If no override is applied, will return null.
See [**EnchantmentGlintOverride**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#enchantment_glint_override) on McWiki for more details.

**Changers**:
- `set` = Allows you to override the glint.
- `reset` = Reset back to default state.
- `delete` = Will delete any value (vanilla or not).

`delete` = Will delete the item model of this item.
`reset` = Will reset the item model back to the vanilla value.

syntax_14702.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14702.title: Formatted Timespan
syntax_14702.description:
Formats a Timespan into a string using a format.
**Available Formats**:
 - `y` = years
 - `M` = months
 - `d` = days
 - `H` = hours
 - `m` = minutes
 - `s` = seconds
 - `S` = milliseconds
 - `'text'` = arbitrary text content
**Note**: It's not currently possible to include a single-quote in a format.
Token values are printed using decimal digits.
A token character can be repeated to ensure that the field occupies a certain minimum size.
Values will be left-padded with 0 unless padding is disabled in the method invocation.

en:
Formats a Timespan into a string using a format.
**Available Formats**:
 - `y` = years
 - `M` = months
 - `d` = days
 - `H` = hours
 - `m` = minutes
 - `s` = seconds
 - `S` = milliseconds
 - `'text'` = arbitrary text content
**Note**: It's not currently possible to include a single-quote in a format.
Token values are printed using decimal digits.
A token character can be repeated to ensure that the field occupies a certain minimum size.
Values will be left-padded with 0 unless padding is disabled in the method invocation.

syntax_13417.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13417.title: NBT - Modify NBT
syntax_13417.description:
Modify NBT of an entity/block(TileEntity)/item.
This method is faster than the old NBT expressions, as the changes aren't applied to the object until the section is done.
This section creates its own internal event, which means previous event-values will not work.
**OPTIONS**:
`entity` = Will modify the base nbt of an entity/player.
`block` = Will modify the base nbt of a block, must be a tile entity block.
`item` = Will modify the base nbt of an item (the components section of an item).
`custom` = Will modify the custom nbt of an item (the "minecraft:custom_data" component), entity or block.

en:
Modify NBT of an entity/block(TileEntity)/item.
This method is faster than the old NBT expressions, as the changes aren't applied to the object until the section is done.
This section creates its own internal event, which means previous event-values will not work.
**OPTIONS**:
`entity` = Will modify the base nbt of an entity/player.
`block` = Will modify the base nbt of a block, must be a tile entity block.
`item` = Will modify the base nbt of an item (the components section of an item).
`custom` = Will modify the custom nbt of an item (the "minecraft:custom_data" component), entity or block.

syntax_13411.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13411.title: Player Fail Move
syntax_13411.description:
Called when a player attempts to move, but is prevented from doing so by the server.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-fail move reason`
   - **Description**: The reason they failed to move.
 - `event-location`
   - **Description**: The location they moved from.
 - `future event-location`
   - **Description**: The location they moved to.
 - `event-boolean (yes/no)`
   - **Description**: Whether the player is allowed to move.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `future event-boolean (yes/no)`
   - **Description**: Whether to log warning to console.
   - **Changers**: SET
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
syntax_13411.event_values: event-boolean (yes/no), event-fail move reason, event-location, event-player, event-world, future event-boolean (yes/no), future event-location

 - `future event-location`
   - **Description**: The location they moved to.
 - `event-boolean (yes/no)`
   - **Description**: Whether the player is allowed to move.

syntax_12893.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_12893.title: ItemComponent - Consumable Component Apply
syntax_12893.description:
Apply a consumable component to an item.
If present, this item can be consumed by the player.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Consumable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#consumable) on McWiki for more info.
**Entries**:
- `consume_seconds` = The amount of time it takes for a player to consume the item. Defaults to 1.6 seconds. [Optional]
- `animation` = The animation used during consumption of the item. [Optional]
  Must be one of "none", "eat", "drink", "block", "bow", "spear", "crossbow", "spyglass", "toot_horn" or "brush". Defaults to "eat"]
- `sound` = A sound key to player when consumed. Defaults to "entity.generic.eat" [Optional]
- `has_consume_particles` = Whether consumption particles are emitted while consuming this item. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `on_consume_effect` = A `consume effect` to by applied to the component (supports a list) [Optional].
- `on_consume_effects` = A section to apply `consume effects` [Optional].
syntax_12893.entries: [{"name": "consume_seconds", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "animation", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "sound", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "has_consume_particles", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "on_consume_effect", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "on_consume_effects", "isRequired": false, "isSection": true}]

en:
Apply a consumable component to an item.
If present, this item can be consumed by the player.
Requires Paper 1.21.3+
See [**Consumable Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#consumable) on McWiki for more info.
**Entries**:
- `consume_seconds` = The amount of time it takes for a player to consume the item. Defaults to 1.6 seconds. [Optional]
- `animation` = The animation used during consumption of the item. [Optional]
  Must be one of "none", "eat", "drink", "block", "bow", "spear", "crossbow", "spyglass", "toot_horn" or "brush". Defaults to "eat"]
- `sound` = A sound key to player when consumed. Defaults to "entity.generic.eat" [Optional]
- `has_consume_particles` = Whether consumption particles are emitted while consuming this item. Defaults to true. [Optional]
- `on_consume_effect` = A `consume effect` to by applied to the component (supports a list) [Optional].
- `on_consume_effects` = A section to apply `consume effects` [Optional].

syntax_10456.title: Experience Orb Merge
syntax_10456.description:
Fired anytime the server is about to merge 2 experience orbs into one.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-merge-source`
   - **Description**: The orb that is subject to being removed and merged into the target orb.
 - `event-merge-target`
   - **Description**: The orb that will absorb the other experience orb.

 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`
syntax_10456.event_values: event-command sender, event-entity, event-entity type, event-location, event-merge-source, event-merge-target, event-world

en:
Fired anytime the server is about to merge 2 experience orbs into one.
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-merge-source`
   - **Description**: The orb that is subject to being removed and merged into the target orb.
 - `event-merge-target`
   - **Description**: The orb that will absorb the other experience orb.
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-command sender`
 - `event-world`
 - `event-location`
 - `event-entity type`

syntax_13416.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_13416.title: ItemComponent - Tooltip Style Component Apply
syntax_13416.description:
Apply a tooltip style component to any item allowing you to hide the tooltip or specific components.
You will also require a `consumable` component to actually make the item consumable.
Requires Paper 1.21.5+
See [**Tooltip Style Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tooltip_style) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `hide_tooltip` = If true, the item will have no tooltip when hovered (optional, boolean).
- `hidden_components` = The tooltips provided by any component in this list will be hidden. If that component provides no tooltip, it will have no effect (optional, data component types).

en:
Apply a tooltip style component to any item allowing you to hide the tooltip or specific components.
You will also require a `consumable` component to actually make the item consumable.
Requires Paper 1.21.5+
See [**Tooltip Style Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#tooltip_style) on McWiki for more details.

**Entries/Sections**:
- `hide_tooltip` = If true, the item will have no tooltip when hovered (optional, boolean).
- `hidden_components` = The tooltips provided by any component in this list will be hidden. If that component provides no tooltip, it will have no effect (optional, data component types).

syntax_11820.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_11820.title: Attribute Modifier - Apply
syntax_11820.description:
Apply an attribute modifier to an item or living entity.
See [**McWiki Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#attribute_modifiers) and [**McWiki Modifiers**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Attribute#Modifiers) for further details.

`transient` = Non-persisent attribute modifier (LivingEntities only, not Items), will not save to the entity's NBT (Requires PaperMC).

**Entries/Sections**:
- `attribute` = The attribute this modifier is to act upon.
- `slot` = EquipmentSlotGroup the item must be in for the modifier to take effect (optional, default = any).
- `id` = The NamespacedKey to identify this modifier.
- `amount` = Amount of change from the modifier.
- `operation` = The operation to decide how to modify.
syntax_11820.entries: [{"name": "attribute", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "slot", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "id", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "amount", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}, {"name": "operation", "isRequired": true, "isSection": false}]

en:
Apply an attribute modifier to an item or living entity.
See [**McWiki Component**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Data_component_format#attribute_modifiers) and [**McWiki Modifiers**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Attribute#Modifiers) for further details.

`transient` = Non-persisent attribute modifier (LivingEntities only, not Items), will not save to the entity's NBT (Requires PaperMC).

**Entries/Sections**:
- `attribute` = The attribute this modifier is to act upon.
- `slot` = EquipmentSlotGroup the item must be in for the modifier to take effect (optional, default = any).
- `id` = The NamespacedKey to identify this modifier.
- `amount` = Amount of change from the modifier.
- `operation` = The operation to decide how to modify.

syntax_14881.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14881.title: Player Track/Untrack Entity
syntax_14881.description:
Called when a player tracks or untracks an entity (Refers to the entity being sent/removed to/from the client).
Adding or removing entities from the world at the point in time this event is called is completely unsupported and should be avoided.
Track: If cancelled entity is not shown to the player and interaction in both directions is not possible.
(This is copied from Paper javadocs and does not seem true. When testing on a zombie, the zombie still attacked me)
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`
syntax_14881.syntax_pattern:
[on] player track entity
[on] player untrack entity

en:
Called when a player tracks or untracks an entity (Refers to the entity being sent/removed to/from the client).
Adding or removing entities from the world at the point in time this event is called is completely unsupported and should be avoided.
Track: If cancelled entity is not shown to the player and interaction in both directions is not possible.
(This is copied from Paper javadocs and does not seem true. When testing on a zombie, the zombie still attacked me)
 
**Event Values:**
 - `event-entity`
 - `event-player`
 - `event-world`

syntax_14875.creator: ShaneBee
syntax_14875.title: ResourcePack - Send Request
syntax_14875.description:
Send a resource pack request to an audience or player connection.
This is similar to the effect but includes a callback section to handle what the client does.
This can be used in the 'Async Player Connection Configure' event as well.

**Entries**:
 - `packs` = A section where you can apply multiple packs to send at once (required).
   - Use the 'ResourcePack - Apply Pack' effect to add packs in this section.
 - `prompt` = The prompt to send to the player. Accepts String/TextComponent/TextComp. [optional]
 - `force` = Whether or not to force the player to accept the resource pack [optional boolean].
 - `received` = A callback section where you can run code when the resource pack is received.
   - This section may be called multiple times for different stages of the request.

**Event Values in Received Section**:
 - `event-uuid` = The id of the resource pack.
 - `event-status` = The status (ResourcePackStatus) of the resource pack request.
 - `event-audience` = The audience of the resource pack request.
syntax_14875.entries: [{"name": "packs", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}, {"name": "prompt", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "force", "isRequired": false, "isSection": false}, {"name": "received", "isRequired": true, "isSection": true}]

en:
Send a resource pack request to an audience or player connection.
This is similar to the effect but includes a callback section to handle what the client does.
This can be used in the 'Async Player Connection Configure' event as well.

**Entries**:
 - `packs` = A section where you can apply multiple packs to send at once (required).
   - Use the 'ResourcePack - Apply Pack' effect to add packs in this section.
 - `prompt` = The prompt to send to the player. Accepts String/TextComponent/TextComp. [optional]
 - `force` = Whether or not to force the player to accept the resource pack [optional boolean].
 - `received` = A callback section where you can run code when the resource pack is received.
   - This section may be called multiple times for different stages of the request.

**Event Values in Received Section**:
 - `event-uuid` = The id of the resource pack.
 - `event-status` = The status (ResourcePackStatus) of the resource pack request.
 - `event-audience` = The audience of the resource pack request.

syntax_10698.creator: eyesniper2
syntax_10698.title: NamespacedKey - Get
syntax_10698.description:
Get the namespaced key of an object or from a string.
Note when getting key from string:
 - NamespacedKeys are a string based key which consists of two components - a namespace and a key (ex: "namespace:key").
 - Namespaces may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, and hyphens.
 - Keys may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes.
 - Minecraft uses the "minecraft:" namespace for built in objects.
 - If a namespace is not provided, the Minecraft namespace will be used by default -> "minecraft:your_key"
 - Keys may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes.
 - Keep an eye on your console when using namespaced keys as errors will spit out when they're invalid.
For more info please check out [**Identifier**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Identifier) on McWiki.

en:
Get the namespaced key of an object or from a string.
Note when getting key from string:
 - NamespacedKeys are a string based key which consists of two components - a namespace and a key (ex: "namespace:key").
 - Namespaces may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, and hyphens.
 - Keys may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes.
 - Minecraft uses the "minecraft:" namespace for built in objects.
 - If a namespace is not provided, the Minecraft namespace will be used by default -> "minecraft:your_key"
 - Keys may only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes.
 - Keep an eye on your console when using namespaced keys as errors will spit out when they're invalid.
For more info please check out [**Identifier**](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Identifier) on McWiki.
